1-1-1

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Statement to Investors

I. Purpose of the Company's Listing

Unitree Technology (宇树科技) is a world-renowned, internationally leading high-performance general-purpose robotics company, dedicated to the R&D, manufacturing, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. The Company was the first in the world to achieve the open commercial sale and industry deployment of high-performance quadruped robots, and has maintained a global leading position in annual sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots and quadruped robots in recent years. The Company's high-performance general-purpose robot products are widely used by numerous well-known domestic and international universities and research institutions, technology enterprises, and global developers, and enjoy a high degree of recognition particularly in overseas markets. With its globally leading market share and continuously breakthrough technological innovations, the Company has driven technological advancement and industrialization of the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry.

Unitree Technology (宇树科技) has achieved steady growth in operating results in recent years. In 2025, while achieving rapid growth in net profit after deducting non-recurring items, the Company also achieved the world's No. 1 in humanoid robot shipments. The Company is keenly aware that current achievements are only a starting point, and the wave of embodied intelligence has only just begun. Unitree Technology (宇树科技) bears the mission of advancing global technological and productivity progress. This is both the Company's dream and the expectation of the general public. Therefore, we hope to gather broader forces through this listing, to move forward hand-in-hand with all investors, to continue to deeply cultivate the core technology R&D and industrial deployment of general-purpose embodied intelligent robots, and to allow intelligent robots to serve all of society sooner and better.

(I) Seizing the Opportunities of the Robotics Industry and Advancing Key Core Technology Research

The Company focuses on independent innovation in core technologies for high-performance general-purpose robots. In the area of quadruped robots, the Company's B-series industrial-grade products and Go-series consumer-grade products have been widely applied in scientific research, industrial inspection, emergency rescue, intelligent services, and other fields. In the area of humanoid robots, since the launch of its first general-purpose humanoid robot H1 in August 2023, the Company has successively self-developed and released four series of humanoid robots (excluding wheeled models), and has achieved breakthrough technological advances and performance in areas such as motion performance and anthropomorphism, impact and drop resistance, product robustness, and cluster scheduling capabilities. Currently, the Company's humanoid robots have been widely applied in scientific research, application development, education and teaching, cultural performances, intelligent services, and other fields.

With the continuous improvement in product sales, the Company is further expanding from a manufacturer of quadruped robots and humanoid robots to a builder of the high-performance general-purpose robotics industrial ecosystem. In response to and to lead the development of global intelligent manufacturing and the artificial intelligence industry, and to accelerate key core technology research and industrial deployment, the Company urgently needs to establish a large-scale, intelligent manufacturing system and to continuously increase R&D investment. This listing aims to strengthen capital capacity in order to continue to enhance the Company's independent innovation capability and comprehensive leading advantages across the entire industrial chain in the field of embodied intelligence, to continuously enrich the product matrix, and to lead the trends of technological innovation and large-scale scenario applications in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry.

(II) Optimizing Corporate Governance and Talent Strategy to Build Momentum for Sustained Development

The general-purpose robotics industry is characterized by intensive talent and technology requirements and rapid innovation iteration. Continuously attracting outstanding talent and improving talent innovation incentives are the core competitiveness of enterprise development. Through this listing, the Company will further improve the modern corporate governance structure, strengthen the Board of Directors' strategic decision-making and risk management functions; implement long-term equity incentive plans and establish long-term talent recruitment and cultivation mechanisms, with a focus on expanding R&D teams in the fields of artificial intelligence, motion control, and perception interaction. The Company will utilize the funds raised in this listing to further strengthen investment in frontier technologies such as embodied intelligence, high-precision control, and reinforcement learning, to consolidate technological advantages, and to create long-term value returns for investors.

II. Establishment and Improvement of the Issuer's Modern Enterprise System

The Company strictly complies with laws and regulations including the Company Law, the Securities Law, and the Guidelines for the Governance of Listed Companies, and has established a tiered governance structure centered on the Shareholders' Meeting, the Board of Directors and its special committees, and the management. The Company has formulated and improved the Articles of Association and other corporate governance and internal control systems, and has established a sound corporate governance structure and modern enterprise system that meets the governance requirements of listed companies and ensures that minority shareholders can fully exercise their rights.

The Company places great importance on the protection of investors' rights and interests. It has established a scientific and transparent profit distribution mechanism, and has committed to continuously sharing the results of the Company's operational development with all investors through long-term and reasonable dividend policies, on the premise of meeting business development needs.

III. Necessity of the Issuer's Current Financing and Plan for Use of Raised Funds

General-purpose robotics is a technology-intensive, R&D-driven industry, and the development and application exploration of both software and hardware technologies are currently in the early stages of rapid development. Based on the Company's own competitive advantages and strategic development needs, the Company has planned the investment projects for the funds to be raised in this offering, namely: the Intelligent Robot Model R&D Project, the Robot Body R&D Project, the New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project, and the Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project. These projects are all centered on the Company's principal business activities and directed toward the field of technological innovation, and will assist the Company's sustained development in terms of enhancing capital capacity, strengthening R&D investment, and improving industrialization capabilities.

IV. Issuer's Ability to Continue as a Going Concern and Future Development Plans

(I) Ability to Continue as a Going Concern

In recent years, the Company has achieved rapid growth in operating results by virtue of its first-mover technological advantage in the field of high-performance general-purpose robots, its continuous technology and product iteration capabilities, and its rapid commercialization capabilities. From January to September 2025, the Company achieved operating revenue of RMB 1,167,490,100 and net profit after deducting non-recurring items of RMB 430,612,300, with a significant improvement in financial condition and sustained profitability.

(II) Future Development Plans

The Company's development goal is to become a continuously leading enterprise in the global high-performance general-purpose robotics field. With independent R&D as its core, after years of accumulation, the Company has established a self-developed and self-produced system covering robot bodies, core intelligent algorithms, embodied intelligence, and core components, forming a rich product matrix that includes core products represented by humanoid robots and quadruped robots, as well as robot components represented by joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and perceptual sensors, and the Company carries out product promotion and industry applications around the aforementioned products.

In the future, the Company will continue to deploy frontier technology R&D. Through the listing, the Company strives to accelerate the high-quality development of the embodied intelligence industrial chain while enhancing its financial capacity, optimizing corporate governance, and improving talent incentives, and to lead the global robotics industry toward large-scale scenario applications through continuous technological innovation.

2026 marks the 10th anniversary of the founding of Unitree Technology (宇树科技). Over these ten years, we have always harbored our original aspirations, dreaming of using technology to advance the progress of human society. At this moment, we are on the eve of a global breakthrough in AI and embodied intelligence technologies — the dawn of humanity's march toward a higher civilization. Once, this dream seemed distant, but through ten years of unremitting effort, we have truly gained the confidence and capability to pursue and realize our dreams. The road ahead is long; we will redouble our efforts and stay the course, living up to all those who have helped and supported Unitree Technology (宇树科技). We sincerely hope to walk alongside all investors, sharing the joy of Unitree Technology's (宇树科技) growth and the fruits of its development. Let us together realize humanity's ultimate dream — AGI!

II. Technical Definitions

Term Definition
High-Performance General-Purpose Robot (高性能通用机器人) refers to An intelligent robot capable of environmental perception, with flexible locomotion capabilities, autonomous learning and collaborative capabilities, and adaptable to different tasks
Artificial Intelligence / AI (人工智能/AI) refers to A new technological science that researches and develops theories, methods, technologies, and application systems for simulating, extending, and expanding human intelligence
Embodied Intelligence (具身智能) refers to A form of intelligence that arises, develops, and achieves autonomous behavior and adaptive capabilities through real-time interaction with the physical world, relying on a physical body
Artificial General Intelligence / AGI (通用人工智能/AGI) refers to A form of intelligence that possesses general cognitive capabilities comparable to those of humans, capable of understanding, learning, and executing intellectual tasks in any domain, and capable of autonomous reasoning, planning, decision-making, and continuous learning in unknown environments
AI Large Model / Artificial Intelligence Large Model (AI大模型/人工智能大模型) refers to An artificial intelligence model based on deep learning and constructed through large-scale data pre-training, with an extremely large number of parameters
Embodied Intelligence Model (具身智能模型) refers to The general term for embodied intelligence technologies related to general-purpose robots, mainly composed of embodied physical intelligence model technology and embodied large model technology
Embodied Physical Intelligence Model (具身本体智能模型) refers to Physical Intelligence (PI) technology that enables robots to achieve highly stable, highly complex, and highly dynamic responsive motion control capabilities, often analogized to the robot's "cerebellum"
Embodied Large Model (具身大模型) refers to Cognitive Intelligence technology that enables robots to achieve capabilities such as understanding interactions, autonomous decision-making, and task planning, often analogized to the robot's "brain"

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Term Definition
Spatiotemporal Modeling (时空建模) refers to The process of simplifying and abstractly representing spatial phenomena; by applying a set of sequential, interactive spatial analysis commands to raw data and derived data, it interprets spatial phenomena or discovers spatial patterns.
Reinforcement Learning (强化学习) refers to A machine learning paradigm in which an agent learns optimal strategies through interaction with an environment; its core lies in value function optimization and long-term reward maximization, and it is widely applied in fields such as gaming and robot control.
Secondary Development (二次开发) refers to The technical process of extending functionality, optimizing performance, or customizing scenarios on existing robotic systems or platforms by calling their open application programming interfaces (APIs), software development kits (SDKs), or underlying frameworks, rather than building a system from scratch.
Motor (电机) refers to An electromagnetic device that converts or transmits electrical energy in accordance with the law of electromagnetic induction, thereby generating driving torque. Motors are regarded as the actuating units of robots and are installed at each joint as needed to control joint movement, directly determining the performance of the robot.
Reducer / Gearbox (减速器) refers to A core transmission device used in the field of robotics that achieves precise transmission control through the ratio of output torque to input torque.
Planetary Reducer (行星减速器) refers to A precision speed-reduction device that employs planetary gear transmission principles, transmitting power through multi-tooth meshing; it features high precision, high rigidity, and high efficiency.
Encoder (编码器) refers to A device that encodes and converts signals (such as bit streams) or data into a form suitable for communication, transmission, and storage. It is primarily used to measure joint angles and positions to ensure the accuracy and stability of robot motion.
Lead Screw / Ball Screw (丝杠) refers to A mechanical transmission element that converts rotary motion into linear motion or linear motion into rotary motion; widely used in machine tools, robots, and automation equipment. Its core structure consists of a screw (rotating component) and a nut (linear-motion component); classified by friction characteristics into sliding lead screws (trapezoidal screws), rolling screws (ball screws), and hydrostatic screws.
Motion Control (运动控制) refers to The control of a robot's precise pose (position and orientation) through algorithmic coordination of core components such as motors, sensors, and mechanical structures.
Environmental Perception (环境感知) refers to The collection of visual, auditory, tactile, and other information via sensor arrays, combined with technologies such as computer vision and pattern recognition to process data, outputting cognitive results that surpass basic sensory capabilities, thereby enabling the robot to understand and adapt to the environment in which it performs tasks.
Human-Robot Interaction (人机交互) refers to The transmission of information between humans and robots via speech recognition, gesture control, touchscreens, and other means; natural language processing, computer vision, and other technologies enable the robot to understand human instructions and ultimately generate responses.
Robotic Arm (机械臂) refers to A mechatronic device that simulates the functions of a human arm, wrist, and hand. It can move any object or tool according to time-varying requirements of spatial pose (position and orientation) in order to accomplish task requirements.
LiDAR / Laser Radar (激光雷达) refers to An active optical remote sensing device that emits laser beams to detect characteristic quantities such as the position and velocity of target objects.
Millimeter-Wave Radar (毫米波雷达) refers to A device that transmits electromagnetic waves in the millimeter-wave band (30–300 GHz) and receives echo signals reflected by targets to calculate the distance, velocity, and angle information of those targets; it features high resolution, all-weather operational capability, and anti-interference characteristics.
Depth Camera (深度相机) refers to A device capable of acquiring depth information about objects in a scene (i.e., the distance from the object to the camera), achieving three-dimensional imaging through technologies such as structured light, time-of-flight, or binocular stereo vision.
Dexterous Hand (灵巧手) refers to A high-degree-of-freedom robotic end-effector that simulates the functions of the human hand; it is a core component of humanoid robots and achieves grasping and manipulation of complex objects through multi-joint, multi-modal sensing, and intelligent control technologies.
Linear Actuator (线性执行器) refers to A mechatronic device that converts input energy (e.g., electrical, hydraulic, or pneumatic energy) into linear displacement output through electronic, electrical, or mechanical control means.
Rotary Actuator (旋转执行器) refers to A mechatronic device that converts input energy (e.g., electrical, hydraulic, or pneumatic energy) into rotary motion through electronic, electrical, or mechanical control means, thereby driving mechanical components to perform circular motion.
Machine Learning (机器学习) refers to A technology that uses algorithms and models to enable computers to automatically learn from data and make predictions or decisions; it is a branch of artificial intelligence.

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Term Definition
Generalization Capability (泛化能力) refers to A core capability of machine learning algorithms, referring to the ability of a model to produce reasonable outputs in scenarios outside the training dataset. In the robotics field, this capability manifests as the robot's ability to recognize and handle previously unseen objects and adapt to changes in the environment.
Robustness (鲁棒性) refers to The ability of a control system to maintain stable performance and functionality in the face of changes to its internal structure and external environment.
SLAM refers to Simultaneous Localization and Mapping; its core is the collection of environmental data through sensors (such as LiDAR, cameras, inertial measurement units, etc.) to simultaneously estimate the robot's own pose and construct an environmental map.
Open-Source Ecosystem (开源生态) refers to A dynamic system based on the principles of openness, sharing, and collaboration; its core is the promotion of the free flow and efficient allocation of technological resources through open-source code. It is a collaborative system composed of participants such as developers, enterprises, and communities, aimed at driving technological innovation and industrial upgrading through sharing and collaboration.
Obstacle Detection (障碍识别) refers to One of the key technologies in the fields of robot navigation, autonomous driving, and intelligent surveillance. Its core objective is to accurately and rapidly detect potential obstacles in complex environments to ensure the safety and reliability of the system.
PCBA refers to Printed Circuit Board Assembly; a functional module formed by mounting electronic components onto a PCB (Printed Circuit Board) through soldering and other processes; it is a core component of electronic devices.
Load / Ultimate Load (载荷/极限载荷) refers to In mechanics and engineering, a load refers to an external force acting on an object or structure that can cause stress, deformation, or internal force; ultimate load is an important parameter in safety engineering for assessing structural failure, defined as the instability load or design load at which the structure enters a plastic state.
Bionics / Bionic Technology (仿生/仿生技术) refers to The study of the structural, functional, energy conversion, and information control characteristics of biological systems and the application of these principles to engineering technology, in order to create more efficient, intelligent, and environmentally friendly technical systems.
OTA / Over-the-Air Upgrade (OTA/空中升级) refers to Over-the-Air Technology; a technology for remotely managing mobile terminal devices and SIM card data via mobile communication air interfaces.

Section II Overview

This overview provides only a brief summary of the full text of the Prospectus. Investors should carefully read the full text of the Prospectus before making an investment decision.

I. Notice of Material Matters

(I) Equity Incentive Arrangements Established Prior to Listing and the Financial Impact of Share-Based Payments

The Company implements an employee stock ownership plan by establishing equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) and its upstream partnership enterprises, and by granting partnership interests in the incentive platform to incentivized employees. During the Reporting Period, in order to maintain the proportional scale of incentive equity, Shanghai Yuyi made two capital contributions to the Company in January 2022 and June 2025, respectively. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Shanghai Yuyi holds 10.94% of the Company's shares.

The Company hereby reminds investors to pay attention to the net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses and the profitability it reflects. In addition to the share-based payment expenses arising from grants to incentive recipients, the two capital contributions by Shanghai Yuyi to the Company resulted in the recognition of share-based payment expenses of RMB 18,722,600 and RMB 349,065,500 in 2022 and 2025, respectively, all of which were charged in full to management expenses in the current period as non-recurring gains and losses. The accounting treatment for these share-based payment expenses does not involve any cash outflow from the Company; while they affect net profit during the Reporting Period, they do not alter the net profit attributable to owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses, nor the cash flow position. For example, the Company's net profit after deducting non-recurring gains and losses for the period from January to September 2025 was RMB 430,612,300, and the reviewed net profit after deducting non-recurring gains and losses for the full year 2025 was RMB 600,104,700.

In accordance with the Company's employee stock ownership plan, all partnership interests in Shanghai Yuyi's upstream partnerships held by Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), the Company's actual controller, will in the future be used entirely for employee equity incentives (Wang Xingxing himself is not an incentive recipient). It is planned that no less than 50% of such interests will be granted within two natural years after the 36th month following the Company's initial public offering and listing, with the remainder to be granted within the remaining effective period of the employee stock ownership plan. The implementation of this equity incentive arrangement will not result in the issuance of new Company shares or the dilution of the interests of new and existing shareholders after listing; the exercise price paid by incentive recipients, after deducting the cost of partnership interest contributions and related taxes and fees, will be delivered to the Company free of charge in its entirety. In the future, the Company will recognize the relevant periodic share-based payment expenses based on the fair value of the shares at the time of grant, the employee grant price, and the agreed service period. Share-based payment expenses may have a significant impact on the Company's operating results in future periods.

(II) Special Risk Warnings

The Company hereby specially reminds investors to carefully read "Section III Risk Factors" of this Prospectus, and to pay particular attention to the following matters:

1. Risk of Technology Breakthroughs and Product Innovation Falling Short of Expectations

The high-performance general-purpose robotics industry in which the Company operates is one of the most complex and cutting-edge technological fields within robotics. As a technology-driven and talent-intensive industry, it integrates numerous disciplines and technical directions including software and hardware algorithms, artificial intelligence reinforcement learning, embodied large models, scene data analysis, and motors/reducers/sensors. Technological innovation in these fields is frequent, upfront investment is high, and uncertainty is significant. Among these, the embodied intelligence technology of general-purpose robots mainly consists of the embodied native intelligence model and the embodied large model, which are often analogized to the "cerebellum" and "brain" of the robot, respectively. The embodied native intelligence model enables the robot to possess highly stable, highly complex whole-body dexterous motion capabilities and forms an important foundation for the robot to autonomously complete complex operational tasks in real environments; the embodied large model enables the robot to possess intelligent interaction capabilities such as understanding the environment, perceiving intent, making autonomous decisions, and executing tasks, and is an important prerequisite for the broader application of general-purpose robots in industrial production, social services, household environments, and other scenarios.

At present, the Company's general-purpose robot products have already integrated a self-developed embodied native intelligence model and possess globally leading native intelligent motion capabilities, and a relatively mature large language model has been integrated into products such as the G1; however, given that embodied large model technology is at the research and development testing stage globally, the Company had not yet applied its self-developed general-purpose embodied large model at scale in robot products during the Reporting Period, but has conducted research, development testing, and deployment verification in pilot scenarios such as its own factory, and has accumulated corresponding technical reserves.

The Company attaches great importance to independent innovation and technological R&D, and will continue to increase investment in embodied large model R&D through the fundraising investment projects of this offering. However, if the Company and the global embodied intelligence industry fail to make important technological advances in the embodied large model field, there will be uncertainty regarding the timeline for large-scale application of general-purpose robots. Furthermore, if the Company fails to accurately grasp industry technology trends, makes erroneous decisions in technology R&D and product development, or fails to achieve breakthroughs in key technologies or meet expected performance metrics, causing the Company's products to fall behind peer technologies, this will have an adverse impact on the Company's sustained competitive advantage and operating results.

2. Risk of Downstream Large-Scale Commercial Application Falling Short of Expectations

The large-scale commercial application of humanoid robots still faces uncertainty, with a risk of progress falling short of expectations. Currently, humanoid robots have limited technological maturity in areas such as the generalization capability of embodied large models and the precision and durability of dexterous hands, and capabilities in refined manipulation, intelligent decision-making, and adaptation to non-standardized scenarios are not yet fully developed. In particular, embodied large model technology is at the research and development testing stage globally; in addition to generalization capability, its stability and reliability also require ongoing verification and improvement. If the relevant technical difficulties cannot be continuously resolved and overcome, the level of intelligence, generalization capability, and working efficiency of general-purpose robot products will be unable to meet commercialization requirements.

On the demand side, apart from specific application scenarios, a rigid demand has not yet formed in the consumer market for general-purpose robots, and the process of building the application ecosystem and the market cultivation cycle are relatively long. At the same time, there are shortcomings in industrial chain supporting capabilities such as the energy systems and real-time computing platforms of general-purpose robots, and product battery life, computing speed, and durability have not yet been verified at scale. In addition, factors such as regulatory policies, industry standards, and social acceptance may also impose constraints on the commercialization process. If progress in technology breakthroughs, cost control, and market cultivation falls short of expectations, the commercial rollout of general-purpose robots will face material delays.

3. Risk of Escalation of International Trade Friction and Regulatory Policies

In recent years, international trade friction has created certain obstacles for Chinese enterprises conducting overseas market business, and the risk of uncertainty in the international trade and overseas commercial environment has increased. In particular, since 2025, the U.S. government's policies on import tariffs on foreign goods have fluctuated, further elevating the uncertainty risk in the international trade and overseas commercial environment. During each period of the Reporting Period, the Company's revenue from overseas sources accounted for more than 35% of total revenue, which is a relatively high proportion. If the United States continues to intensify the implementation of trade, tariff, and other policies that are significantly unfavorable to Chinese companies' export operations, or places the Company on lists restricting procurement cooperation or controlling technology exports, the Company faces the risk of being unable to sustain high growth in overseas sales and may even experience a decline in performance as a result.

In addition, during the Reporting Period, the Company procured imported materials through domestic agents, accounting for approximately 20% of total raw material procurement. In view of the uncertainty in industrial trade policies and the international political situation, if adverse changes occur in external supply chains and overseas market regulatory policies, compounded by further escalation of relevant U.S. trade restriction and export control policies, this may have an adverse impact on the Company's procurement of imported materials and technical cooperation.

4. Risk of Intensified Industry Competition and Disorderly/Improper Competition

With the accelerated deployment of global technology giants and the widespread emergence of start-up enterprises, market competition in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry is intensifying further. International enterprises such as Tesla, which possess large-scale mass production capabilities and artificial intelligence technology resources, may exert a potential impact on market participants including the Company through the large-scale mass production and technological evolution of their humanoid robot products. These competitors, leveraging their algorithmic advantages accumulated in fields such as autonomous driving, supply chain management experience, and economies of scale, are expected to rapidly reduce the unit cost of their humanoid robots such as Optimus, which will directly intensify price competition in the industry and create potential pressure on the Company's product pricing, market share, and profit margin levels.

Furthermore, the core of industry competition is migrating from hardware manufacturing toward full-stack "perception-decision-execution" technology. If the Company fails to continuously increase R&D investment, or fails to effectively leverage its first-mover advantages accumulated in high-performance general-purpose robotics and other fields to rapidly achieve deep penetration into vertical industries and build a stable customer base, it will face operational risks of having its core technological advantages eroded and its market position adversely affected in the competitive market. Although the Company has formulated corresponding technology development paths and market expansion strategies, there is uncertainty as to whether the measures to address the aforementioned risks can be effectively implemented and what their ultimate results will be.

5. Corporate Governance Risk Arising from the Special Voting Rights Mechanism

Prior to this offering, Wang Xingxing, the Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller, in aggregate controls 34.7630% of the Company's shares; through the special voting rights arrangement, Wang Xingxing in aggregate controls 68.7816% of the Company's voting rights. Under the special voting rights mechanism, the actual controller is able to determine ordinary resolutions of the Company's general meeting of shareholders and can play a similarly determinative role with respect to special resolutions of the general meeting of shareholders, significantly enhancing the actual controller's material influence over decisions made by the Company's general meeting of shareholders. After this offering, the proportion of voting rights in aggregate controlled by Wang Xingxing will decrease to no more than 65.3090%. In special circumstances, the actual controller's interests may not be aligned with those of the Company's other shareholders, and there is a possibility of harm to the interests of other shareholders, particularly minority shareholders.

6. Risk of Decline in High Revenue Growth Rate and Gross Margin Level

From 2023 to 2025, the Company's revenue from principal operations grew year-on-year by 29.95%, 145.83%, and 335.31% (reviewed), respectively, while the gross margin on principal operations for the same periods was 44.22%, 56.41%, and 60.27% (reviewed), respectively; both revenue growth rates and gross margins have been gradually increasing and remain at relatively high levels. The Company's revenue growth rate is jointly determined by factors including the overall state of industry development, the status of large-scale commercial downstream applications, and the competitiveness and market reputation of the Company's products; fluctuations in gross margins across various business lines are also jointly influenced by multiple factors including changes in product mix, shifts in the proportion of various business models, and changes in unit prices and costs. If future changes occur such as intensified industry market competition, technological upgrades, or product iteration, the Company may face a more complex operating environment. For example, in terms of product application, if the pace of commercial application expansion falls short of expectations, or if the Company's product technology fails to meet downstream market requirements, the Company's revenue growth may slow; in terms of industry competition, if the Company is unable to maintain sustained technological leadership in its products, homogeneous competition and inventory overhang may result in product price declines; in terms of project implementation, if the effectiveness of the Company's production line automation transformation and fundraising investment projects falls short of expectations, the Company may be unable to sustain cost reduction and efficiency improvement efforts, or may experience rising costs driven by depreciation and amortization of newly added equipment and facilities. Any of these circumstances could lead to a decline in the Company's future gross margins, adversely affecting its profitability and performance.

IV. Principal Business Operations of the Issuer

(I) Principal Business, Principal Products or Services, and Their Applications

Unitree Technology (宇树科技) is a world-renowned and internationally leading high-performance general-purpose robotics company, focusing on the R&D, production, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. The Company was the first in the world to achieve public sales and industry deployment of high-performance quadruped robots, and in recent years has maintained leading global sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots and quadruped robots. The Company uses technological innovation to build its robot product ecosystem; its humanoid and quadruped robots are widely used by numerous well-known domestic and foreign universities, scientific research institutions, technology companies, and global developers, and are particularly well recognized in overseas markets. With its globally leading market share and continuously advancing technological innovation, the Company has driven technological progress and the industrialization process of the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry.

The Company attaches great importance to independent R&D and technological innovation. While continuously iterating in R&D to enrich its humanoid robot and quadruped robot body product lines, it has conducted full-stack R&D of core robot model algorithms including embodied intelligence, reinforcement learning, and motion control; intelligent systems including thermal management, energy management, and motor drive; and core robot components including high-performance motors, reducers, dexterous hands, LiDAR, and various sensors. The Company insists on full-stack independent development of high-performance general-purpose robots and core components, has significantly improved the integration and whole-body dexterous motion capabilities of general-purpose robots, and has continuously led technological innovation in the general-purpose robotics industry.

During the Reporting Period, no material changes occurred in the Company's principal business. The composition of principal business revenue for each period is as follows:

(II) Principal Raw Materials and Major Suppliers

The Company's required raw materials mainly include various mechanical components, electronic components, and electrical materials. For information on major suppliers, please refer to "IV. (II) Procurement from the Top Five Raw Material Suppliers" under "Section V Business and Technology" of this Prospectus. The Company adopts a dynamic procurement model based on "safety stock" levels, has established a strict qualified supplier admission system, and conducts supplier admission and day-to-day management taking into account factors such as delivery time, quality, cost, and service. At the same time, the Company has established multiple supply channels for the same type of raw material to ensure the stability of raw material supply.

(III) Overview of Principal Production Model

The Company adopts a production planning model of "production-to-sales plus safety stock." Each month, the Company organizes production, supply, and sales meetings based on sales plans and product inventory levels; formulates an overall production plan based on the sales plan; breaks down the production plan into specific production tasks and issues them to each production department; and the production departments organize and arrange production based on the production tasks. In terms of production methods, the Company adopts a production and organization model that combines in-house assembly of complete machines and core components with external procurement and processing of parts and certain processes — that is, complete robot machines and core components are produced and assembled within the Company, while non-core parts and certain processes are handled through customized procurement and outsourced processing. The foregoing production model ensures that the Company can effectively control all key core segments of the product production process and achieve the optimal balance between technological advancement and cost effectiveness.

(IV) Principal Sales Methods, Channels, and Key Customers

The Company's products cover application fields including scientific research, industry, and consumer use; downstream application scenarios are extensive and customer needs are diverse. Combining different sales channels and product delivery models, the Company has adopted a sales model that integrates offline and online channels, as well as direct sales and distribution.

The Company's high-performance general-purpose robots, by virtue of their excellent product performance, provide critical support for frontier robotic algorithm verification, system integration research, and embodied intelligence exploration. They are widely recognized and used by academic and technological enterprise communities, with representative customers including numerous well-known domestic and foreign universities, research institutions, and industry-leading technology enterprises.

(V) Industry Competition and the Issuer's Competitive Position in the Industry

The Company insists on full-stack independent development of core technologies for general-purpose robots and has continuously enriched its product matrix, leading innovation and large-scale deployment in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry. It has achieved a major share of the global quadruped robot and humanoid robot markets, with particularly high brand recognition and market share in overseas markets. During the Reporting Period, the Company's cumulative sales of quadruped robots exceeded 30,000 units, establishing the Company's dominant position in the global quadruped robot market. At the same time, since the Company independently developed and launched its first humanoid robot, the H1, in 2023, and the mid-sized humanoid robot G1 in 2024, humanoid robot product sales have achieved rapid growth. In the full year 2025, the Company's humanoid robot shipments exceeded 5,500 units (pure humanoid robots, excluding wheeled dual-arm robots), ranking first globally in shipments, demonstrating that the Company has achieved a leading market position in the general-purpose robotics field through core independent R&D driving commercial first-mover advantage.

In terms of performance, since its founding, the Company has achieved outstanding results in technological innovation in the high-performance general-purpose robotics field and has continuously broken numerous industry performance records. For example, in 2020, the A1 quadruped robot achieved a maximum running speed of 3.3 meters per second, making it the fastest small-to-medium-sized quadruped robot domestically at that time; in 2021, the Go1 quadruped robot improved running speed to 4.7 meters per second, setting a world record for running speed among quadruped robots of similar specifications at that time. In the humanoid robot field, the Company's first humanoid robot H1, released in 2023, successfully completed the world's first electrically driven in-place back flip of high difficulty the following year, and in 2025 achieved a running speed exceeding 5 meters per second, breaking the world record for full-size humanoid robots.

Since 2024, the Company's humanoid robot motion control and swarm scheduling performance has continued to improve. Following the inauguration of full-size humanoid robot autonomous swarm performances in 2024, in early 2025, 16 H1 humanoid robots participated in the CCTV Spring Festival Gala dance performance "YangBOT" (《秧BOT》) in a fully AI-driven swarm configuration. In March 2025, the G1 humanoid robot achieved an electrically driven in-place side flip, again breaking industry records; in May of the same year, the G1 humanoid robot became the sole participating model in the mecha combat arena competition of the "CMG World Robot Championship · Series Competition" (《CMG世界机器人大赛•系列赛》), opening a new global field of humanoid robot combat competition; in August, at the inaugural World Robot Games, the H1 and G1 humanoid robots performed outstandingly — H1 won first place in the 1,500-meter run, first place in the 400-meter run, and first place in the 4×100-meter relay race successively, while G1 won first place in the 100-meter obstacle course with a time of 33.71 seconds, together winning 11 medals in total; the Company was the company with the most gold medals and the most total medals, fully demonstrating the motion performance and reliability advantages of the Company's products in high-dynamic tasks. By early 2026, the Company set a new benchmark for humanoid robot technology with the fully autonomous swarm-controlled martial arts performance "WuBOT" (《武BOT》), in which 24 G1 and 1 H2 robots demonstrated millisecond-level coordination in high-speed parkour and drunken-fist sparring, breaking multiple world records including continuous fancy table-flip parkour and launch backflips.

In terms of scenario applications, the Company's general-purpose robots have achieved large-scale application in numerous industry-level and consumer-level markets. On one hand, the Company has promoted the expanded application of general-purpose robots in inspection and surveying, fire rescue, public services, and other fields globally, bringing more efficient, safe, and intelligent new solutions to industries such as energy and chemical engineering, intelligent fire protection, and smart cities. On the other hand, the Company has adopted a "high performance + high value for money" strategy to broadly cover the scientific research and consumer markets for general-purpose robots, promoting the application of general-purpose robots in scientific research, education and teaching, cultural performances, intelligent services, and other fields, and has achieved an industry-leading market share.

Section 3      Risk Factors

I. Risks Related to the Issuer

(I) Risk That Technological Breakthroughs and Product Innovation Fall Short of Expectations

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Key Technology Leakage and Loss of Core Personnel

Through years of technological innovation and accumulated experience, the Company has developed a series of independently researched core technologies surrounding its high-performance general-purpose robot business and has rapidly risen to a leading position in the industry. The Company's independently developed core components and algorithm systems — including high-performance motors, reducers, motion control systems, and various sensors — have reached industry-leading levels, providing a high-performance and cost-effective body foundation for the research, development, and commercial application of general-purpose robots globally. At the same time, under the leadership of founder Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), the Company has assembled a core research and development team with a solid foundation in self-developed technologies, an entrepreneurial and innovative culture, extensive project experience, and leading R&D capabilities.

The above core technological achievements and core R&D team are key to the Company's ability to maintain its industry-leading competitiveness. As competition in the industry intensifies, the competition for talent among enterprises and regions is also becoming increasingly fierce. If the Company experiences leakage of key core technologies or loss of core R&D personnel, this will have an adverse impact on the Company's technological innovation capability, market competitiveness, and operating results.

(III) Corporate Governance Risk Arising from the Shareholders' Special Voting Rights Mechanism

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(IV) Risk of High Revenue Growth Rate and Declining Gross Margin Level

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(V) Risk of Rapid Growth in Inventory Scale and Inventory Write-Down Losses

At the end of each period during the reporting period, the book value of the Company's inventory was RMB 77,537,400, RMB 78,957,700, RMB 140,580,500, and RMB 278,036,600, respectively, accounting for 24.12%, 24.20%, 11.07%, and 11.14% of current assets, respectively. The amounts have been continuously increasing and the proportions are relatively high. The balance of inventory write-down provisions was RMB 6,525,000, RMB 9,710,700, RMB 16,793,700, and RMB 22,810,600, respectively, with provision ratios of 7.76%, 10.95%, 10.67%, and 7.58%, respectively. If changes occur in raw material prices, the supply chain, and the market environment, or if the unit price of the Company's principal products undergoes adverse changes due to product upgrades, supply-demand dynamics, or other factors, resulting in a significant decline in the net realizable value of relevant products in the Company's inventory, the Company will face the risk of increased inventory write-downs affecting its operating performance.

(VI) Risk of Rapid Growth in Accounts Receivable and Bad Debt Losses

At the end of each period during the reporting period, the balance of the Company's accounts receivable was RMB 7,846,700, RMB 11,067,100, RMB 21,594,800, and RMB 83,456,500, respectively, representing 6.38%, 6.95%, 5.50%, and 7.15% of revenue for the corresponding periods, respectively. During the reporting period, the Company's business grew rapidly, and the scale of accounts receivable grew accordingly. The Company's principal accounts receivable counterparties are domestic and foreign universities, well-known technology and industrial enterprises, or long-term cooperative customers, all of which are in good operating condition and have high credit standing. In the future, as the Company's business scale expands, accounts receivable may continue to increase. If the financial condition of downstream customers deteriorates, there is a risk that accounts receivable may become uncollectible, which could have an adverse impact on the Company's operating performance.

(VII) Risk of Significant New Capital Investment and Expenditures from Fundraising Investment Projects

During the implementation of the fundraising investment projects in this offering, significant amounts of depreciation, amortization, and other expenditures will be incurred, which will have a certain impact on the Company's future operating performance. Since the construction, completion, and generation of returns from the fundraising investment projects require a certain period of time and are subject to various uncertainties, if the future development trend of the industry in which the Company operates, market conditions, or other factors undergo materially adverse changes, resulting in the fundraising investment projects failing to achieve their projected returns, the additional depreciation, amortization, and period expenses arising from the fundraising investment projects will have an adverse impact on the Company's future operating performance.

(VIII) Risk of Foreign Exchange Rate Fluctuations Related to Foreign Currency Settlement of Overseas Revenue

During the reporting period, the Company's overseas principal business revenue amounted to RMB 69,352,800, RMB 87,642,400, RMB 215,707,300, and RMB 452,786,200, representing 57.21%, 55.63%, 55.70%, and 39.20% of principal business revenue, respectively. During the reporting period, the Company's exchange gains and losses amounted to RMB -5,678,200, RMB -2,981,200, RMB -4,437,700, and RMB 14,228,200, respectively. During January to September 2025, benefiting from the significant increase in domestic revenue, the proportion of overseas revenue declined somewhat; however, its sales amount and year-on-year growth rate still achieved strong growth, with overseas revenue still accounting for more than 35% of the Company's principal business revenue. The Company's overseas sales are primarily settled in US dollars. If the exchange rate of the US dollar against the Renminbi becomes more volatile in the future, or if exchange rate policies undergo significant changes, this will have a certain impact on the Company's performance.

(IX) Risk of Declining Operating Performance Under Multiple Influencing Factors

During the reporting period, the Company's operating performance achieved rapid growth. In 2024 and January to September 2025, operating revenue was RMB 392,370,600 and RMB 1,167,490,100, respectively, and the net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit and loss was RMB 77,503,600 and RMB 430,612,300, respectively. Benefiting from the accelerated development of the general-purpose robot industry, the continued breakthroughs in the performance of the Company's core products, the continuous enrichment of the product line, and the ongoing expansion of application scenarios, the Company achieved substantial growth in both operating revenue and profit levels during the reporting period. If in the future the Company is affected by factors such as slower-than-expected development of new technologies or promotion of new products, rising raw material costs, increased R&D investment intensity, and large share-based payment expenses arising from newly granted substantial equity incentives, and the Company fails to take effective measures to respond to such changes in a timely manner, the Company will face the risk of a significant decline in operating performance or even losses.

II. Industry-Related Risks

(I) Risk That Large-Scale Downstream Commercial Applications Fall Short of Expectations

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Escalating International Trade Friction and Control Policies

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(III) Risk of Intensifying Industry Competition and Disorderly and Improper Competition

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(IV) Risk of Social Attention Hot Topics and Market Demand Volatility

As a frontier technology sector, high-performance general-purpose robots have received extensive and intense attention from capital markets, the industry, and social media in recent years, with generally optimistic market expectations. However, the industry as a whole is still in its early stages of technological breakthroughs and commercial exploration. If progress is slow in areas such as key technology breakthroughs, cost control, or scenario applications, the current market enthusiasm and capital attention may decline significantly, causing the overall development pace of the industry to fall short of expectations and thereby having an adverse impact on the Company's market expansion and operating performance.

In the long run, the large-scale commercial application of humanoid robots needs to overcome multiple factors including technical reliability, production costs, market acceptance, and laws and regulations. If the cultivation of downstream application market demand lags, or if phased or cyclical fluctuations in demand occur, this will directly affect the industry's and the Company's capacity absorption and revenue realization. At the same time, changes in relevant global industrial policies and laws and regulations may also create uncertainty in market demand.

(II) Establishment of the Joint Stock Company

On May 6, 2025, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting, which approved the audit and appraisal of the net assets of Unitree Limited with March 31, 2025 as the reference date for audit and appraisal, and the conversion of the entire Unitree Limited into a joint stock limited company through the conversion of audited net assets into shares.

On May 12, 2025, Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants (容诚会计师) issued Audit Report No. "Rongcheng Shen Zi [2025] 230Z3996" (容诚审字[2025]230Z3996号). Upon verification, as of the audit reference date of March 31, 2025, the net assets of Unitree Limited were RMB 1,371,618,138.76. On the same date, Zhongshui Zhiyuan (中水致远) issued Asset Appraisal Report No. "Zhongshui Zhiyuan Ping Bao Zi [2025] No. 020447" (中水致远评报字[2025]第020447号). Upon appraisal, as of the appraisal reference date of March 31, 2025, the appraised value of the net assets of Unitree Limited was RMB 1,432,959,000.

On May 15, 2025, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting, which approved the conversion of the entire Unitree Limited into Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司) as a joint stock limited company by all shareholders of Unitree Limited acting as promoters; the conversion used the audited net assets of Unitree Limited as of March 31, 2025 of RMB 1,371,618,138.76 as the basis, converted into 2,594,223 shares at a ratio of 1:0.0019, with a par value of RMB 1 per share (registered capital of RMB 2,594,223, with the registered capital remaining unchanged before and after the conversion), and the remaining RMB 1,369,023,915.76 was recorded as capital surplus of the joint stock company. On the same date, all shareholders of Unitree Limited, acting as promoters, jointly signed the Promoter Agreement.

On May 16, 2025, Unitree Technology (宇树科技) convened the Inaugural General Meeting and First Extraordinary General Meeting, at which resolutions were passed on matters including the Proposal Regarding the Conversion of the Company in Its Entirety into a Joint Stock Company, the Proposal Regarding the Work Report on the Preparation of the Joint Stock Company, and the Proposal Regarding the Formulation of the Articles of Association, and the non-employee directors and non-employee supervisors of the first Board of Directors were elected. On the same date, all promoters of the Company signed the Articles of Association.

On May 26, 2025, Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants (容诚会计师) issued Capital Verification Report No. "Rongcheng Yan Zi [2025] 230Z0052" (容诚验字[2025]230Z0052号). Upon verification, as of May 16, 2025, Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (in preparation) had received registered capital of RMB 2,594,223 in aggregate paid in by all shareholders, with the method of contribution being net assets, of which: RMB 2,594,223 was recorded as share capital and the remainder was recorded as capital surplus.

On May 28, 2025, Unitree Limited completed the business registration change procedures for the conversion of the entire company into a joint stock limited company, and obtained the Business License issued by the Hangzhou High-tech Industry Development Zone (Binjiang) Market Supervision and Administration Bureau with Unified Social Credit Code 91330108MA27YJ5H56.

VIII. Special Voting Rights Shares or Similar Arrangements of the Issuer

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has special voting rights shares in place. The details are as follows:

(I) Basic Information on the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

1. Shareholders' Resolution on the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

On May 16, 2025, the Company held the inaugural general meeting and the first extraordinary general meeting of the joint stock company, at which the resolution "Proposal Regarding the <Plan for the Establishment of Special Voting Rights Shares by the Joint Stock Company>" was voted upon and passed, approving the establishment of special voting rights shares. Pursuant to the special voting rights arrangement, 44,074,296 shares held by the Company's actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) are designated as Class A special voting rights shares, carrying special voting rights; all remaining shares of the Company are Class B ordinary shares, which do not carry special voting rights.

Except for matters stipulated in the Company's Articles of Association, when shareholders exercise voting rights on matters considered at the general meeting of shareholders, each special voting rights share carries 10 votes, and each ordinary share carries 1 vote.

2. Duration of the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

After the Company establishes the special voting rights arrangement, unless the general meeting of shareholders resolves to terminate the arrangement, it shall continue to subsist and operate in perpetuity in accordance with the relevant laws, regulations, and the Company's Articles of Association.

3. Holder Eligibility

Shareholders holding Class A shares shall be persons who have made significant contributions to the Company's development or business growth, and who continuously serve as directors of the Company, or holding entities actually controlled by such persons. The aggregate shareholding of shareholders holding Class A shares in the Company shall reach at least 10% of all issued shares with voting rights. The Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) meets the foregoing requirements.

4. Ratio of Voting Rights of Special Voting Rights Shares to Ordinary Shares

Prior to this offering, the number of Class A shares with special voting rights is 44,074,296, held by the actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), and the remaining 319,943,610 shares are Class B ordinary shares, with details as follows:

5. Scope of General Meeting Matters in Which the Special Voting Rights Held by the Holder May Participate in Voting

In accordance with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association, when holders of Class A shares and Class B shares vote on all resolutions submitted to the general meeting of shareholders for voting, each Class A share carries 10 votes and each Class B share carries 1 vote. When shareholders exercise voting rights on the following matters, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share:

  1. Amendments to the Company's Articles of Association;
  2. Changes to the number of voting rights carried by special voting rights shares;
  3. Appointment or removal of independent directors;
  4. Appointment or removal of members of the audit committee;
  5. Appointment or removal of the accounting firm that issues audit opinions on the periodic reports of the listed company;
  6. Division, spin-off, merger, dissolution, and liquidation of the Company.

A resolution of the general meeting of shareholders on item (2) above shall be passed by no less than two-thirds of the voting rights held by the shareholders present at the meeting; provided, however, that this shall not apply to the conversion of a corresponding number of Class A shares into Class B shares in accordance with the Company's Articles of Association and the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules.

6. Lock-up Arrangements and Transfer Restrictions for Special Voting Rights Shares

(1) No Issuance of Additional Class A Shares

After the Company's shares are listed on a domestic securities exchange, except in the case of pro-rata rights issues or capitalisation of reserves, no special voting rights shares shall be issued domestically or overseas, and the proportion of Class A shares shall not be increased. If, due to share repurchases or other reasons, the proportion of Class A shares increases, the Company shall simultaneously take measures such as converting a corresponding number of Class A shares into Class B shares to ensure that the proportion of Class A shares does not exceed the original level.

(2) Transfer Restrictions on Class A Shares

Class A shares of the Company may not be traded on the secondary market, but may be transferred in accordance with the relevant rules of the stock exchange.

(3) Conversion of Class A Shares

Upon the occurrence of any of the following circumstances, Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares on a 1:1 basis:

  1. The shareholder holding Class A shares no longer meets the qualifications and minimum shareholding requirements stipulated in the Company's Articles of Association, or loses the relevant ability to perform their duties, resigns, or dies;
  2. The shareholder actually holding Class A shares loses actual control over the relevant holding entity;
  3. The shareholder holding Class A shares transfers the Class A shares held to another party, or entrusts another party to exercise the voting rights attached to the Class A shares;
  4. A change of control of the Company occurs.

Upon the occurrence of item (4) in the preceding paragraph, all Class A shares already issued by the Company shall be converted into Class B shares. Upon the occurrence of item (1) in the preceding paragraph, Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares from the time the relevant circumstance occurs; the relevant shareholder shall immediately notify the Company, and the Company shall promptly disclose the specific circumstances, the time of occurrence, the number of Class A shares converted into Class B shares, the remaining number of Class A shares, and other relevant information.

(II) Relevant Risks Potentially Arising from Special Voting Rights and Their Impact on the Company

Under the special voting rights mechanism, the Company's actual controller is able to determine ordinary resolutions at the general meeting of shareholders and can also exert significant influence on special resolutions at the general meeting of shareholders, thereby further enhancing the actual controller's influence over the Company's major decisions through the general meeting of shareholders. If minority shareholders, including public investors, oppose the actual controller on significant corporate decisions and vote against such decisions at the general meeting of shareholders, they may be unable to materially affect the outcome of the vote due to the relative disparity in the number of votes per share. In special circumstances, the interests of the actual controller may not be consistent with those of the Company's other shareholders, particularly minority shareholders. The special voting rights arrangement established by the Company carries the possibility of affecting the interests of other shareholders, particularly minority shareholders.

(III) Investor Protection Measures

1. Measures to Prevent Abuse of the Special Voting Rights Mechanism

In order to prudently establish and operate the special voting rights mechanism and prevent its abuse, the Company has put in place effective constraints on special voting rights and on shareholders holding special voting rights through the following measures:

(1) Strictly Limiting the Scope of Special Voting Rights in Accordance with Law

The Company establishes special voting rights in order to enhance the stability and continuity of its own operational strategy as a science and technology innovation enterprise. The boundaries of special voting rights are clearly defined and do not apply to certain significant matters relating to fundamental rights of investors.

In accordance with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association, when the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on specific significant matters, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share. For details, please refer to "Section VIII, (I), Item 5: Scope of General Meeting Matters in Which the Special Voting Rights Held by the Holder May Participate in Voting" of this section.

(2) Imposing Strict Reduction Restrictions on Special Voting Rights Shares

Class A shares with special voting rights are subject to stricter reduction restrictions than Class B shares, causing Class A shareholders to place greater emphasis on the long-term, stable development of the Company relative to other shareholders holding Class B shares and public investors, thereby constraining behavior that might abuse special voting rights to the detriment of the Company's interests.

In accordance with the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, Class A shares of the Company may be transferred in accordance with relevant exchange rules, but may not be traded on the secondary market. When a Class A shareholder transfers Class A shares to another party, the Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares on a 1:1 basis.

(3) Broad, Prudent, and Independent Deliberation and Voting Procedures for Establishing Special Voting Rights

Prior to convening the extraordinary general meeting to deliberate on the proposal to establish special voting rights, all shareholders of the Company thoroughly consulted on the content and implications of the special voting rights mechanism. When the extraordinary general meeting deliberated on the proposal to establish special voting rights, the proposal was voted on by all shareholders with voting rights and received unanimous approval from all shareholders.

2. Measures and Institutional Arrangements for Protecting Investors' Rights and Interests

In addition to the above measures to prevent abuse of the special voting rights mechanism, the Company has also established the following specific systems and measures to fully protect investors' rights and interests:

(1) Emphasis on Shareholders' Right to Dividends

The Company has reviewed and passed the "Proposal Regarding the Formulation of the Company's Three-Year Shareholder Dividend Return Plan Following the Initial Public Offering and Listing on the Science and Technology Innovation Board" at the ninth meeting of the first Board of Directors and the sixth extraordinary general meeting of shareholders in 2025. When the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on specific cash dividend plans, it shall fully solicit the opinions and demands of minority shareholders, adhere to the basic principle of prioritising cash dividends, and, in the absence of significant investment plans or major cash expenditures, distribute annual cash dividends of no less than 10% of the distributable profits realised in the current period.

(2) Giving Full Play to the Supervisory Functions of Independent Directors

The Company has appointed 3 independent directors and established the System for the Work of Independent Directors, granting independent directors special powers including proposing the convening of board meetings, requesting the convening of extraordinary general meetings, and independently engaging external audit institutions and advisory institutions, and providing that they have the right to issue independent opinions on significant matters. When the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on the appointment or removal of independent directors, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share, which is conducive to strengthening the role of independent directors in representing the interests of minority shareholders and exercising independent supervision.

(3) Minority Shareholders' Right to Nominate Directors and to Convene Extraordinary Board Meetings

In accordance with Article 83 of the Articles of Association, shareholders holding or collectively holding 1% or more of the Company's shares with voting rights may propose candidates for directors to the Board of Directors, and the Board of Directors shall submit such candidates to the general meeting of shareholders for deliberation.

In accordance with Article 117 of the Articles of Association and Article 123 of the Articles of Association (Draft), shareholders representing more than 1/10 of voting rights, more than 1/3 of directors, the Chairman of the Board, the audit committee, or a majority of independent directors may propose the convening of an extraordinary board meeting. The Chairman of the Board shall convene and preside over an extraordinary board meeting within 10 days of receiving the proposal.

(4) Establishing a Series of Systems Including Standardized Related-Party Transactions

The Company has lawfully established a series of systems including the Rules of Procedure for General Meetings of Shareholders, Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors, Related-Party Transaction Management System, Financing and External Guarantee Management System, External Investment Management System, and System for the Work of Independent Directors, so as to improve corporate governance. By placing the approval authority for larger transactions and important matters at the level of the general meeting of shareholders, the Company has formed a multi-tier decision-making mechanism comprising management decision-making, board of directors review and approval, audit committee supervision, and general meeting of shareholders review and approval, thereby preventing management from engaging in improper conduct that may harm the interests of the Company and public investors.

(5) Strengthening Information Disclosure and Investor Relations Management

The Company will strictly comply with the information disclosure regulatory requirements set forth in the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, the Measures for Continuous Supervision of Science and Technology Innovation Board Listed Companies (Trial), and the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Listed Company Self-Regulatory Guidelines No. 1 — Standardized Operations. The ninth meeting of the first Board of Directors reviewed and passed the Information Disclosure Management System, which sets out clear provisions on the principles, procedures, authority, responsibilities, confidentiality measures, and contact information for information disclosure. In addition, the Company will disclose in its periodic reports the implementation and changes of the special voting rights arrangement during the reporting period, as well as the implementation of measures relating to the protection of investors' lawful rights and interests under the special voting rights arrangement.

2. Termination of Special Rights

In September 2025, the Company and its existing shareholders entered into the Termination Agreement Regarding Special Shareholder Rights Clauses of Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter the "Termination Agreement"), which stipulated: with respect to the repurchase rights enjoyed by each shareholder, as well as other VAM-type clauses and/or arrangements meeting any of the following conditions: 1) the Company itself is a party to the VAM arrangement; 2) there exists an agreement that may result in a change of control of the Company; 3) the arrangement is linked to the Company's market capitalization; or 4) there exist circumstances that seriously affect the Company's ability to continue as a going concern or otherwise seriously impair investors' interests — the Termination Agreement provides that the repurchase right and similar clauses shall be thoroughly terminated and void ab initio as of the day immediately preceding the date on which the audit report for the Company's listing application is issued, and shall not be reinstated.

The Company's auditor, RSM China (容诚所), issued the audit report for this offering application on October 15, 2025. Accordingly, the shareholder repurchase right clauses were thoroughly terminated and void ab initio prior to the filing, and shall not be reinstated.

In order to treat all investors fairly and better protect the rights and interests of minority investors, the Company and its existing shareholders entered into the Supplemental Agreement to the Termination Agreement Regarding Special Shareholder Rights Clauses of Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter the "Supplemental Agreement") in January 2026, making supplementary arrangements regarding the termination of other special shareholder rights clauses held by investor shareholders. The other special shareholder rights clauses were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement and shall not be reinstated. The specific contents are as follows:

All special shareholder rights other than the repurchase right (including information and inspection rights, pre-emptive rights, rights of first refusal, co-sale rights, anti-dilution rights, dividend rights, priority liquidation rights, drag-along rights, priority investment rights, most-favored-nation clauses, special rights regarding material matters of the shareholders' meeting and board of directors, special rights of investor directors, share transfer restrictions, director appointment rights (including board observer rights), and supervisor appointment rights) were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement. The termination of such clauses is permanent, unconditional, irrevocable, and non-reinstatable.

Upon review, the sponsor, the issuer's legal counsel, and the issuer's auditor are of the opinion that the repurchase rights enjoyed by the investor shareholders have been thoroughly terminated and void ab initio as of the day immediately preceding the date on which the audit report for the Company's listing application was issued (October 15, 2025), and shall not be reinstated; the other special rights clauses enjoyed by the investor shareholders were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement in January 2026, and shall not be reinstated. Accordingly, the Company has effected a non-reinstatable, thorough termination of the special rights clauses of investor shareholders, which complies with the relevant regulatory requirements of the Guidelines for Application of Regulatory Rules — Issuance Category No. 4.
