XII. Equity Incentive Plans and Related Arrangements and Implementation

In order to further improve the Company's incentive system, motivate employees who have made significant contributions to the Company, attract outstanding talent, establish a medium- and long-term incentive and restraint mechanism, and effectively align the interests of shareholders, the Company, and individual incentive recipients so as to promote the Company's long-term development, the Company has, prior to the filing of this public offering application, formulated equity incentive-related arrangements in the form of an employee stock ownership plan. Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) and its upper-tier partnership enterprises Hangzhou Yixin (杭州翌心) and Hangzhou Yiyi (杭州翌意) serve as the shareholding platforms for the Company's equity incentives. Incentive recipients indirectly hold the Company's shares by holding partnership interests in the incentive platforms as limited partners.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Shanghai Yuyi holds 39,828,600 shares of the Company, representing a shareholding ratio of 10.9414%. For the basic information of Shanghai Yuyi, please refer to "Section VII, (II), 1, Shanghai Yuyi" of this section.

(I) Implementation of the Equity Incentive Plan

From September 2017 to December 2021, the Company successively entered into Option Agreements (hereinafter the "First Batch Subscription Agreements") with 17 employees including Yang Zhiyu, granting such employees the right to subscribe for equity interests in the Company at a price of RMB 1 per unit of registered capital (hereinafter the "First Equity Incentive").

In January 2024, the shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited (宇树有限) deliberated and approved the Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. 2023 Option Incentive Plan (hereinafter the "Second Equity Incentive" and the "2023 Incentive Plan"), resolving to use Tianjin Unitree (天津宇树) (former name of Shanghai Yuyi) as the equity incentive implementation platform, and to incorporate the First Equity Incentive into the 2023 Incentive Plan for unified implementation and management. The Incentive Plan provides that the equity interests of the Company held by the incentive platform shall serve as incentive equity; incentive employees shall indirectly hold incentive equity by holding a certain quantity of property interests in the incentive platform; and the effective period of the incentive plan is 10 years.

From March 2024 to July 2025, pursuant to the 2023 Incentive Plan, the Company successively granted incentive interests to 6 employees and entered into Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. Option Subscription Agreements (hereinafter the "Second Batch Subscription Agreements").

In order to further improve the Company's equity incentive plan, in May 2025, the board of directors and shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited resolved to confirm the exercise implementation status of the Company's First Equity Incentive and again clearly resolved to incorporate the First Equity Incentive and the Second Equity Incentive for unified implementation and management under the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi.

In June 2025, the Company convened its Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025, which deliberated and approved the proposal regarding the capital increase by the Company's equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi. At this point, the proportion of the Company's shares corresponding to the partnership interests in Shanghai Yuyi available for granting equity incentives increased to 10.9414%.

In September 2025, the Eighth Session of the First Board of Directors and the Fifth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025 deliberated and approved the Proposal on Amending the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter and the amended Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, revising the Company's 2023 Incentive Plan into the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter. Based on the above shareholders' meeting resolutions and the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, the Company entered into supplemental agreements with incentive employees regarding the subscription agreements.

In March 2026, the Fourteenth Session of the First Board of Directors and the First Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2026 deliberated and approved the Proposal on Amending the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter and the amended Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, adjusting the form of granting and exercising equity incentives from a method of one-time grant with staged exercise to restricted shares with one-time grant and exercise but staged release of restrictions. This amendment will take effect with respect to future incentive matters.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, among the incentive recipients who have been granted interests, 9 employees have subsequently resigned, and the corresponding granted incentive interests have been cancelled. Based on the total number of shares after the transfer of capital reserve to share capital in June 2022 and June 2025, the number of Company shares corresponding to the granted incentive interests of 14 employees totals 5,926,576 shares, of which the number of Company shares corresponding to the exercised incentive interests of 11 employees totals 5,113,372 shares. Among the incentive employees who have been granted interests, 12 are from the R&D department, and 2 are from the sales and production departments, respectively.

(II) Arrangements Related to the Equity Incentive Plan

Pursuant to the Company's Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, the main arrangements of the employee stock ownership plan established prior to this offering are as follows:

1. Incentive Interests

Under the above employee stock ownership plan, the equity incentive interests are the partnership interests in the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi and its upper-tier partnership enterprises Hangzhou Yixin (杭州翌心) and Hangzhou Yiyi (杭州翌意).

2. Unlock Period Arrangements

Incentive recipients shall unlock the granted incentive interests in four tranches. If an incentive recipient meets the performance targets within the assessment year, they may unlock 25% of the total granted incentive interests upon the first anniversary of the grant date, a further 25% upon the second anniversary, a further 25% upon the third anniversary, and a further 25% upon the fourth anniversary. If an incentive recipient fails to meet the performance assessment targets for a given year, the unvested interests for that year shall not be carried forward to subsequent years for unlocking.

3. Service Period Arrangements

Prior to a qualifying listing, incentive recipients may not transfer, encumber, or otherwise dispose of all or any part of their incentive interests in the incentive platform; after a qualifying listing, if at the relevant time the Company has been listed for 36 months following the qualifying listing and the unlocked incentive interests for each tranche have been held for 5 years from the date of that tranche's unlock, the incentive recipient may apply to dispose of the incentive interests held. That is, the service period for each tranche of unlocked incentive interests is the later of: 36 months after the qualifying listing and 5 years after the exercise of each respective tranche.

4. Repurchase of Incentive Interests

Pursuant to the relevant provisions of the Company's employee stock ownership plan charter, the handling of incentive interest repurchases under different circumstances is as follows:

(1) Blameless Departure During the Service Period

If an incentive recipient's employment is terminated during the service period due to mutual agreement with the Company, incapacity for work, death, disability, retirement, or the Company's economic layoffs, the repurchase price for the exercised incentive interests held by such recipient shall be calculated according to the following formula:

Repurchase Price = Exercise Unit Price × Number of Incentive Interests × (1 + Applicable Benchmark Interest Rate for Time Deposits of Equivalent Term at the Time × 150% × N), where N is the coefficient equal to the actual number of days from the incentive recipient's unlock date to the repurchase date divided by 365.

(2) Unauthorized Departure or Termination of Service by an Employee Who Has Voluntarily Proposed to Dissolve the Employment Relationship During the Service Period

If an incentive recipient who has voluntarily proposed to dissolve the employment relationship during the service period abandons their post or terminates service prior to reaching a mutual agreement with the Company, the administrator has the right to designate a specific purchaser to repurchase the incentive interests held by such recipient, at a repurchase price equal to the actual consideration paid by the recipient.

(3) Misconduct by an Incentive Recipient or Participation in Other Incentive Plans

If an incentive recipient engages in misconduct or participates in the incentive plan of another company, the administrator has the right to designate a specific purchaser to repurchase the incentive interests held by such recipient, at a repurchase price equal to the actual consideration paid by the recipient.

(III) Impact of Equity Incentives on the Company's Operations, Financial Condition, and Control, and Exercise Arrangements After Listing

1. Accounting Treatment of Share-Based Payments Related to Equity Incentives

The Company's equity incentives involve share-based payment accounting treatment. Share-based payment expenses recognized on an allocated basis over the employees' vesting periods are recorded as recurring profit or loss. The relevant accounting treatment complies with the requirements of the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises and other applicable standards.

Grant Date: The specific grant date agreed upon in the relevant incentive agreements entered into between the Company and the incentive recipients.

Vesting Period: The service period for each tranche of exercised incentive interests is the later of 36 months after the qualifying listing and 5 years after the exercise of each respective tranche, with share-based payment expenses recognized by monthly amortization.

Fair Value: The Company's equity incentives are valued by reference to the second category of restricted shares, using the Black-Scholes model to calculate the fair value of the incentive interests based on the investment price of institutional investors at a comparable point in time. Zhongshui Zhiyuan Asset Appraisal Co., Ltd. (中水致远资产评估有限公司) assessed the fair value of the equity instruments and issued the Value Analysis Report on the Fair Value of Stock Options Involved in the Implementation of Option Incentives by Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (Reference No. 中水致远评咨字[2025]第020114号) and the Value Analysis Report on the Fair Value of Stock Options Involved in the Implementation of Option Incentives by Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (Reference No. 中水致远咨报字[2025]第020019号).

Treatment of Departed Employees: During the vesting period, when an incentive recipient departs, unexercised incentive interests are cancelled, and the share-based payment expenses previously recognized for the departed employee are reversed in the period of departure.

2. Share-Based Payment Situation During the Reporting Period and Future Impact of Share-Based Payments

During the reporting period, share-based payment expenses arising from grants to incentive recipients and recorded as recurring profit or loss were RMB 293,800, RMB 498,000, RMB 979,400, and RMB 1,519,000, respectively, representing a relatively small proportion of the non-recurring items-adjusted net profit for each period. The implementation of the above equity incentives is lawful and compliant. The controlling shareholder and actual controller of the Company have not changed before or after the implementation of the equity incentives, and there has been no material adverse impact on the Company's operations, financial condition, or control, nor is there any situation that damages the interests of the Company or its shareholders.

In addition to the share-based payment expenses arising from grants to incentive recipients recorded as recurring profit or loss, the Company recognized share-based payment expenses of RMB 18,722,600 and RMB 349,065,500 in 2022 and January–September 2025, respectively, in connection with Shanghai Yuyi's two capital increases at registered capital price in January 2022 and June 2025, both of which were recognized in full as non-recurring profit or loss for the respective periods. This caused the Company's accounting net profit to decrease to RMB -22,100,500 and RMB 105,331,400 for 2022 and January–September 2025, respectively. These share-based payment expenses do not involve any cash outflow from the Company. Although they affect the net profit during the reporting period, they do not change the net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit or loss, nor the cash flow situation. The Company's net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit or loss for 2022 and January–September 2025 was RMB -8,070,800 and RMB 430,612,300, respectively.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the partnership interests in the upper-tier partnerships of Shanghai Yuyi held by the Company's actual controller Wang Xingxing correspond in aggregate to a 9.54% equity interest in the Company. These partnership interests will be used in the future to grant equity incentives to the Company's employees. At that time, the Company will calculate and recognize relevant share-based payment expenses for the applicable periods based on the fair value of the shares at the time of granting incentive interests, the employees' grant price, and the agreed service periods. The relevant share-based payment expenses may have a significant impact on the Company's future operating results.

(IV) Nominee Shareholding and Restoration Arrangements for Equity Incentives

1. Nominee Shareholding in the Company's Historical Development

(1) Establishment of the Nominee Shareholding Arrangement

From September 2017 to December 2021, the Company entered into the First Batch Subscription Agreements with the incentive employees under the First Equity Incentive. In view of confidentiality considerations regarding the identity of those incentive recipients, the agreements provided that the Company's equity interests corresponding to the exercise of rights by such incentive recipients would be held in nominee capacity by the Company's actual controller, Wang Xingxing. From March 2018 to April 2025, pursuant to the First Batch Subscription Agreements, the Company's equity interests corresponding to the exercises by 4 First Equity Incentive recipients were successively held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing; by April 2025, the proportion of the Company's equity held in nominee capacity for exercised interests represented 0.56% of the Company's total share capital at that time.

(2) Termination of the Nominee Shareholding Arrangement

In order to further improve the Company's equity incentive plan, in May 2025, the board of directors and shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited resolved to confirm the exercise implementation status of the Company's First Equity Incentive and clearly resolved to incorporate the First Equity Incentive and the Second Equity Incentive for unified implementation and management under the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi. In the same month, the Company entered into Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements with each of the First Equity Incentive recipients respectively, stipulating that the incentive equity corresponding to the recipients' exercises would all be adjusted to the corresponding property interests in the incentive platform, and Wang Xingxing would no longer hold the Company's equity in nominee capacity for the incentive recipients; the matters relating to the incentive recipients' equity incentives would be incorporated into the 2023 Incentive Plan and Shanghai Yuyi for unified implementation and management.

As a result, the nominee shareholding arrangement between the exercised incentive employees and Wang Xingxing in respect of the Company's equity corresponding to the exercised incentive equity was terminated. The First Equity Incentive recipients, upon exercise, will have the corresponding exercised property interests in the incentive platform held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, and will no longer directly or by nominee hold Company equity.

2. Nominee Holding of Property Interests in Shanghai Yuyi's Historical Development

(1) Establishment of the Nominee Holding Arrangement

In 2024, the Company successively entered into Second Batch Subscription Agreements with 3 employees pursuant to the 2023 Incentive Plan. Pursuant to the Second Batch Subscription Agreements, the incentive recipients would not be registered as named partners of the incentive platform with the registration authority upon acquiring incentive interests; the exercised incentive interests would be held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, a partner of the shareholding platform.

At the same time, after the First Equity Incentive recipients signed the Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements in May 2025, the exercised incentive interests of the First Equity Incentive recipients were held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, a partner of the incentive platform. As of September 2025, the equity ratio of the Company's shares corresponding to the partnership interests held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing in the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi on behalf of the aforementioned 11 exercising incentive employees was 1.40%.

(2) Termination of the Nominee Holding Arrangement

In September 2025, the aforementioned 11 employees and the Company entered into Second Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements and Supplemental Agreements to the Second Batch Subscription Agreements, providing that from the effective date of such supplemental agreements and upon the completion of the industrial and commercial change registration/filing for the named registration of the exercised incentive interests, the nominee holding arrangement for the exercised incentive interests between the incentive recipients and the actual controller would be terminated. In November 2025, the exercising employees completed the industrial and commercial change registration, and the nominee holding arrangement for the partnership interests corresponding to 1.40% of the Company's equity in the Shanghai Yuyi shareholding platform was thereby fully terminated.

Based on interviews with the aforementioned persons and written confirmation letters issued by them, the nominee shareholding arrangements in the Company's historical development and the nominee holding of property interests in the historical development of the equity incentive platform have been fully terminated, and the parties to the nominee arrangements have confirmed that no disputes or potential disputes exist during the nominee holding period or in the course of terminating the nominee holding arrangements.

(II) Labor Outsourcing

During the Reporting Period, the Company engaged in labor outsourcing arrangements. Outsourced personnel were primarily involved in assembly and testing work of a highly repetitive nature and relatively low technical complexity within the production and assembly process, as well as auxiliary production activities such as warehousing management and cleaning. For each period of the Reporting Period, the Company's labor outsourcing expenses were RMB 14,003,900, RMB 11,616,900, RMB 19,222,600, and RMB 50,401,100, respectively. The Company's order volume maintained rapid growth during the Reporting Period, and self-recruitment could not meet the rapidly growing labor demand in a short period of time. To ensure product delivery, the Company adopted labor outsourcing as a means of expanding its production capacity. Going forward, the Company plans to alleviate production labor demand through the construction of automated production lines, and the scale of labor outsourcing is expected to gradually decrease as production line construction progresses.

Section 5 — Business and Technology

I. Overview of the Issuer's Principal Business, Main Products and Services

(I) Principal Business and Main Products

1. Overview of Principal Business

Unitree Technology (宇树科技) is a globally renowned and internationally leading high-performance general-purpose robotics company, dedicated to the research and development, production, and sale of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. The Company was the first in the world to achieve public commercial sales and industry-level deployment of high-performance quadruped robots, and has maintained global leading sales volumes in high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots and quadruped robots in recent years. The Company builds a robot product ecosystem through technological innovation; its humanoid and quadruped robots are widely used by numerous renowned domestic and international universities and research institutions, technology companies, and global developers, and enjoy high recognition particularly in overseas markets. Through its globally leading market share and continuously advancing technological innovation, the Company has driven industry-wide technological progress and the commercialization process of high-performance general-purpose robots.

The Company places great emphasis on independent research and development and technological innovation. While continuously iterating on and enriching its humanoid and quadruped robot hardware products, the Company has conducted full-stack R&D of core model algorithms for robots including embodied intelligence, reinforcement learning, and motion control; intelligent systems for thermal management, energy management, and motor drive; and core robot components including high-performance motors, reducers, dexterous hands, LiDAR sensors, and various other sensors. The Company adheres to full-stack self-development of high-performance general-purpose robots and their core components, which has significantly enhanced the integration level and whole-body dexterous motion capabilities of general-purpose robots, and has continuously led technological innovation in the general-purpose robotics industry.

The Company's high-performance general-purpose robots, by virtue of their outstanding product performance, provide important support for frontier algorithm validation, system integration research, and embodied intelligence exploration in robotics, and exert significant influence in the fields of scientific research and technology development related to general-purpose robots worldwide. At the same time, the Company has promoted industry-level applications of general-purpose robots globally in areas such as inspection and surveying, fire-fighting and rescue, and public services, bringing more efficient, safe, and intelligent new solutions to industries including energy and chemicals, intelligent fire safety, and smart cities. In addition, the Company, through its "high-performance + high cost-effectiveness" strategy, was the first to open the critical path from technology development to large-scale application for general-purpose robots, significantly lowering the barrier to entry for consumer-level users, facilitating the application of general-purpose robot products in consumer-level markets such as education, cultural performances, and intelligent services, and winning widespread user favor through outstanding product experience and scenario adaptability, thereby achieving industry-leading market share and brand influence.

2. Main Products

With independent R&D at its core and built upon years of accumulation, the Company has established a self-developed and self-manufactured technology system encompassing robot bodies, intelligent algorithm systems, and core components. This has resulted in main product lines represented by humanoid robots and quadruped robots, as well as core robot components including joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and perception sensors, around which the Company conducts product promotion and industry application.

(1) Humanoid Robots

In August 2023, the Company launched its first self-developed full-size humanoid robot, H1, marking the Company's formal entry into the humanoid robot sector. This robot features the highest dynamic performance among electrically actuated humanoid robots of comparable specifications globally at that time.

In May 2024, the Company released its first self-developed mid-size humanoid robot, G1, which features a more anthropomorphic structural design and degree-of-freedom layout, higher integration, more diverse achievable motions, and richer human-robot interaction capabilities. Subsequently, the Company continuously upgraded G1's walking and running gaits and complex motion capabilities through model algorithm iterations, further enhancing its motor performance and anthropomorphism, enabling it to more smoothly and stably complete long-sequence complex movements and swarm coordination such as dance, boxing, and martial arts, with significantly enhanced product robustness. G1 was the world's best-selling humanoid robot from 2024 to 2025.

In July 2025, the Company released an all-new self-developed small-to-medium-sized humanoid robot, R1. As the Company's lightweight robot product, R1 is capable not only of performing high-difficulty complex movements such as running, boxing, headstands, and rolling, but also features voice and image multimodal interaction capabilities. The R&D and mass production of R1 aim to provide individual consumers, AI developers, educational institutions, and technology enthusiasts with a high-performance, cost-effective, and highly open development platform, to further promote the technological development and ecosystem development of humanoid robots. R1 was named one of TIME magazine's Best Inventions of 2025.

In October 2025, the Company released its second self-developed full-size general-purpose humanoid robot, H2, featuring a 31-degree-of-freedom anthropomorphic configuration capable of precisely replicating highly dynamic complex movements. H2 also features proportions and dimensions close to those of a real human, making it better suited for the deployment of various embodied intelligence models and empowering diverse work scenarios, thereby driving humanoid robots to continuously explore application boundaries and further enriching the Company's product matrix.

(2) Quadruped Robots

Since 2017, the Company has successively developed and mass-produced multiple quadruped robot models, including: Laikago, the Company's first motor-driven quadruped robot, in 2017; AlienGo, the second quadruped robot featuring an entirely new power system and integrated body design, in 2019; A1, an all-new high-performance, cost-effective quadruped robot, in 2020; Go1, the first-generation consumer-grade quadruped robot, in 2021; B1, a new-generation industry-grade quadruped robot, in 2022; Go2, a new-generation consumer-grade quadruped robot, and B2, a new-generation industry-grade quadruped robot, in 2023; B2-W, an industry-grade four-wheeled-legged robot, in 2024; and A2, a new-generation industry-grade mid-size quadruped robot, in 2025.

Among these, the Company's consumer-grade quadruped robots are primarily used in fields such as scientific research and development, education and teaching, consumer entertainment, and intelligent services, while industry-grade quadruped robots are primarily used for inspection, operation, rescue, surveying, and related scientific research and application development in vertical industries such as energy and chemicals, intelligent manufacturing, fire and emergency response, public services, and smart cities. (II) Principal Operating Models

During the Reporting Period, no material changes occurred in the Company's principal operating models. The specific details are as follows:

1. Procurement Model

The Company implements a dynamic procurement model based on "safety stock" and has established a stringent qualified supplier admission system, conducting supplier admission and day-to-day management by comprehensively considering factors such as lead time, quality, cost, and service. To ensure continuity of production and operations, the Company conducts demand forecasting based on actual orders and market changes, plans production tasks in advance, converts them into purchase orders to replenish inventory, and dynamically adjusts inventory based on actual production and sales conditions. At the same time, the Company has established multiple supply channels for the same type of raw materials to ensure stability of raw material supply. In addition, to reduce overall procurement costs, the Company flexibly adjusts procurement quotas based on prices offered by different suppliers.

2. Production Model

The Company adopts a production planning model of "sales-driven production + safety stock," organizing monthly production-supply-sales meetings based on sales plans and product inventory levels to formulate overall production plans. The production plans are broken down into specific production tasks and issued to each production department, which then organizes and arranges production based on the assigned tasks.

The Company adopts a production organization model that combines in-house assembly of complete machines and core components with outsourced procurement and processing for certain parts and processes. That is, complete robot units and core components are produced and assembled within the Company, while non-core components and certain processes are handled through customized procurement and external contract processing. For most mechanical components, the Company adopts a customized procurement model and implements strict incoming inspection for each batch of custom-made parts to ensure that the quality and specifications of externally procured custom parts meet the Company's technical requirements. For most PCBA soldering, injection molding, and certain machining processes, the Company adopts an external contract processing model, whereby contract manufacturers perform the processing based on raw materials and relevant technical requirements provided by the Company. This production model ensures that the Company can effectively control all key core stages of the production process, achieving optimal balance between technological advancement and cost-effectiveness.

3. Sales Model

The Company's products cover both industry-grade and consumer-grade applications, with a wide range of downstream use scenarios and diverse customer types. Combining different product sales channels and delivery methods, the Company adopts a sales model integrating offline and online, as well as direct sales and distribution. The specific details are as follows:

Sales Model Main Content Primary Customer Types
Offline Direct Sales The Company's sales department is organized by downstream application industry and region, engaging directly with end customers through processes such as customer visits, trade show interactions, on-site inspections, and contract signing to establish cooperative relationships; the Company delivers products or solutions directly to customers Domestic and international technology companies, research institutions, universities, etc.
Offline Distribution The Company has established a distribution network through agents both domestically and internationally, leveraging agents' channel and geographic advantages to broaden customer acquisition sources and deepen regional coverage. The Company sells products to domestic and international agents, who then sell the Company's products to downstream customers through their own sales channels. The Company manages agents comprehensively by specifying distribution territories, sales targets, and other criteria Domestic and international agents
Online Direct Sales The Company conducts online sales directly to online customers through e-commerce platforms. Under this model, online customers browse and place orders and make payment through the e-commerce platform, and the Company ships goods directly to customers Main e-commerce platforms include Tmall, JD.com, Shopify, and Amazon
E-commerce Inventory-In The Company ships products to designated warehouses of the e-commerce platform, which then sells them to end customers. The Company's direct customers are the e-commerce platforms. During the Reporting Period, the Company primarily used this model with JD.com Self-Operated and JD.com Joybuy JD.com Self-Operated, JD.com Joybuy

4. R&D Model

The Company has long been committed to the research, development, and technological exploration of high-performance general-purpose robots. Through years of innovation and accumulation, it has established a broad and efficient independent R&D system, and by virtue of its strong R&D technical capabilities and continuous innovation capacity, has rapidly ascended to an industry-leading position.

The Company implements a full-stack independent R&D model, with R&D activities covering the robot body; embodied intelligence models including embodied body intelligence models and embodied large models; functional module systems including energy management, computing platforms, motion control systems, and perception systems; and core components including high-performance motors, reducers, dexterous hands, and LiDAR sensors. Through full-stack independent R&D spanning multiple software and hardware domains and all stages, the Company has significantly enhanced robots' integration level and whole-body dexterous motion capabilities, ensuring continuous iterative updates of its main products and the rapid commercialization of core technologies.

The Company has established a comprehensive R&D organizational management system and conducts R&D activities through its R&D department. The R&D department's primary responsibilities include analyzing and forecasting industry technology development directions, formulating R&D plans based on R&D objectives, and executing R&D activities. R&D projects are subject to corresponding reviews at key stages including project initiation, engineering testing, and project completion, ensuring orderly day-to-day R&D management and technological innovation.

(III) Evolution of Principal Business, Main Products or Services

1. Technology Foundation Period: 2016–2019

The Company's founding and development originated from breakthrough efforts in the core technologies of quadruped robots. From its establishment in 2016, the Company focused on the electric motor-driven technology approach, independently developing the motor drive system, overall mechanical structure, and whole-body control system of quadruped robots. Building upon the XDog prototype developed by the founder in earlier research, the Company reconstructed and developed the Company's first commercial product "Laikago" from scratch, marking the formal commercial delivery of the Company's quadruped robot products. By 2019, the founder led the team to successfully develop high-torque motor technology and independently developed and released the high-dynamic-performance quadruped robot AlienGo, propelling the motor performance of quadruped robots to the international frontier. During this stage, the Company placed great emphasis on technology accumulation, established the full-stack self-development model for complete machines and core components in the hardware domain, and continuously innovated and optimized the motion control algorithms for quadruped robots, establishing the Company's technologically leading position in the quadruped robotics field domestically and internationally.

2. Market Development Period: 2020–2023

During this stage, the Company gradually pioneered commercial applications of quadruped robots across multiple fields through continuous technological innovation and product launches. In 2020, the Company self-developed and mass-produced the next-generation quadruped robot A1 and for the first time brought the product price down to the consumer-grade range. In 2021, Go1 entered the mass consumer market at a retail price of RMB 16,000. In 2022, the Company self-developed the next-generation industry-grade quadruped robot B1 and successfully deployed it in industrial inspection. In 2023, Go2 brought the Company's quadruped robot retail price below RMB 10,000 for the first time at a price of RMB 9,997, and the Company released the further performance-enhanced industry-grade quadruped robot B2. At this point, the Company established a "consumer-grade + industry-grade" dual-track product matrix — with consumer-grade products focusing on scenarios such as scientific research and education, cultural tourism, and sports, and industry-grade products focusing on scenarios such as environmental inspection and emergency rescue — creating synergistic effects of technology sharing and market stratification. Leveraging full-stack self-development of core components to drive cost control and supply chain integration, the Company achieved rapid large-scale mass production of quadruped robots. With the Company's products achieving technological leadership and widespread adoption in both consumer-grade and industry-grade markets, the Company has captured a major share of the global quadruped robot market.

In 2023, the Company extended its core technologies — including motion control algorithms and self-developed core components — to humanoid robots, and self-developed Unitree Technology's (宇树科技) first full-size humanoid robot product H1 within the industry, marking the Company's formal technology and product expansion into the humanoid robot sector.

3. Industry Expansion Period: 2024 to Present

Since 2024, the Company has entered an industry expansion period, achieving multi-dimensional breakthroughs with embodied intelligence technology at its core. At the product and technology level, the Company developed the mid-size humanoid robot G1 with a starting price below RMB 100,000, making it the Company's first mid-size general-purpose humanoid robot to be delivered to the market at scale. The industry-grade B2-W wheeled-legged robot, through reinforcement learning, is capable of autonomously adapting to complex terrain, greatly expanding the operational radius of quadruped robots. At the commercial application level, the Company further built out a business system of "hardware products + scenario solutions + ecosystem services," using the "quadruped robot + humanoid robot" product matrix as a fulcrum to promote the development and application of general-purpose robots in areas such as scientific research and education, commercial consumption, and industry applications. At the model R&D level, following the open-source release of two self-developed embodied large models, UnifoLM-WMA-0 and UnifoLM-VLA-0, which achieved industry-leading test results and industry recognition, in early 2026 the Company's self-developed industrial embodied large model UnifoLM-X1-0 completed pilot deployment and testing in the Company's own factory, capable of autonomously completing tasks such as joint motor assembly, representing a major breakthrough in the global deployment and validation of embodied large models.

As the scale of product sales continues to increase, the Company is further expanding from a manufacturer of quadruped robot and humanoid robot products to a constructor of a high-performance general-purpose robotics industry ecosystem. The Company is building an ecosystem around its own products, open-sourcing full-process algorithms based on the Company's products covering data collection, model training, real-machine deployment, and motion control, to attract global developers to participate in algorithm optimization and general-purpose robot research and development.

II. Basic Conditions and Competitive Landscape of the Industry in Which the Issuer Operates

(I) Industry to Which the Company Belongs and Basis for Determination

The Company is focused on the research and development, production, and sale of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. Its main products include humanoid robots and quadruped robots as its principal product lines, as well as core robot components such as joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and perception sensors, around which the Company conducts product promotion and industry application.

In accordance with the Industrial Classification for National Economic Activities (GB/T4754-2017), the Company's principal business belongs to "C34 General-Purpose Equipment Manufacturing" — specifically "C349 Other General-Purpose Equipment Manufacturing" — "C3499 Other General-Purpose Equipment Manufacturing Not Elsewhere Classified."

In addition, pursuant to the Classification of Strategic Emerging Industries (2018) issued by the National Bureau of Statistics, the Company's principal business belongs to "2 High-End Equipment Manufacturing Industry" — "2.1 Intelligent Manufacturing Equipment Industry" — "2.1.4 Other Intelligent Equipment Manufacturing," as well as to "2 New Generation Information Technology" — "2.5 Artificial Intelligence Industry" — "2.5.2 Artificial Intelligence Hardware" under the Guidance Catalogue of Key Products and Services for Strategic Emerging Industries (2016 Revision).

(III) Industry Development and Future Trends

1. The Role and Position of High-Performance General-Purpose Robots in the Industrial Chain and Their Linkages with Upstream and Downstream Industries

A high-performance general-purpose robot is an intelligent mechatronic platform with high robustness, stability, and reliability, possessing the potential to exceed human capability boundaries in terms of payload, speed, and dynamic performance. By virtue of its multi-degree-of-freedom structural design and agile locomotion capabilities, it is capable of operating across multiple scenarios and traversing complex terrain, and is progressively developing artificial intelligence that integrates perception, decision-making, and interaction, thereby enabling collaboration with the human social environment.

The industrial chain in which the Company's high-performance general-purpose robots are situated mainly consists of various upstream components, midstream high-performance general-purpose robot complete-machine platform suppliers, and downstream terminal applications, and can be divided into two major directions: quadruped robots and humanoid robots.

Among these, the quadruped robot industry has entered the early commercial stage of application exploration, with market applications such as inspection and emergency response growing rapidly. Stimulated by the potential market space, the number of enterprises and institutions deploying quadruped robots globally continues to increase, and an upstream-downstream industrial chain of initial scale has been formed. Humanoid robots are currently in the early commercial stage of technology exploration; commercial deployment in downstream application fields is being accelerated through exploration and verification; technology development and application of certain components in the humanoid robot field are progressing rapidly; and the relevant supply chain is being continuously and rapidly built out. As the humanoid robot innovation ecosystem is progressively established and key technologies continue to break through, China is expected to gradually form an efficient and reliable industrial chain and supply chain system for high-performance general-purpose robots.

The industrial chain of high-performance general-purpose robots is as follows:

1-1-106

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

The upstream of the high-performance general-purpose robot industrial chain includes perception and interaction systems, control systems, motion execution systems, and other components. The specific composition of each part is as follows:

Category Description
Perception and Interaction System The robot perception and interaction system is used to enable the robot's perception and to further realize functions such as learning, decision-making, and control. This component is responsible for perceiving the external environment and simulating the human cognitive decision-making process; its primary functions are environmental understanding, intelligent interaction, and cognitive reasoning. Depending on its functions and application scenarios, the robot is equipped with various types of sensors, including visual sensors, auditory sensors, olfactory sensors, tactile sensors, and inertial navigation systems, to perceive and analyze the external environment and respond to its own state, enabling subsequent complex interactions and operational tasks.
Control System The robot control system refers to the core algorithms and control systems that govern the robot's movement, comprising a series of algorithms and hardware devices. Based on information received from various sensors and actuators, it adjusts the robot's motion state in a timely manner, aiming to enable the robot to achieve autonomous, precise, and efficient motion control and balance regulation, including real-time adjustment of parameters such as attitude, position, and velocity. It must also possess a certain degree of learning and adaptive capability, so as to adjust its control strategy in response to changes in the environment and assigned tasks.
Motion Execution System The carrier and foundation for the robot to realize its actual functions, primarily consisting of actuators, dexterous hands, and new material structural components; this is a critical element affecting the hardware cost and motion performance of the robot. Based on considerations of cost, technical performance, and software-hardware coupling, there are certain differences among robot manufacturers in terms of motion execution system solutions and design details.
Other Comprises the robot's power management system, thermal management system, communications system, and other components.

Throughout the entire industrial chain, high-performance general-purpose robots have relatively high requirements for system integration. Through full-stack in-house development, enterprises can achieve a closed-loop technology cycle spanning perception, decision-making, and control, driving technological synergy and advancement. This not only helps build core technology barriers but also strengthens control over the industrial chain, thereby supporting rapid product iteration and ecosystem development. At the software level, enterprises with core in-house capabilities in motion control, artificial intelligence algorithms, and other areas will command the robot's key control systems, thereby influencing to a certain extent the technological evolution trajectory of high-performance general-purpose robots. At the hardware level, the segments with the most prominent incremental opportunities primarily include components such as sensors, reducers, motors, batteries, lead screws, and bearings. Relevant enterprises need to strengthen independent design and R&D to ensure product consistency and delivery efficiency, and by enhancing rapid-response and large-scale delivery capabilities, expand market share and gradually establish brand recognition and first-mover advantages.

The midstream of the high-performance general-purpose robot industrial chain consists of complete-machine platform providers, which are primarily responsible for the definition, design, manufacturing, and delivery of products such as quadruped robots and humanoid robots. This segment is the core of the industrial chain and is key to driving the industrialization and commercialization of high-performance general-purpose robots. Complete-machine platform providers, by building a well-developed development ecosystem, supply downstream applications with robot tools possessing high robustness, high stability, and high reliability, and play a guiding role in the industry's technology pathway and development direction. Currently, the high-performance general-purpose robot body industry as a whole is in the early stage of industrialization development, and the specific pathway to large-scale commercial deployment remains in the exploratory stage. Different enterprises continue to iterate on component selection, software system development, body design, and application scenario focus, with various technical solutions advancing in parallel. As artificial intelligence, intelligent control, high-precision sensors, and other software and hardware technologies develop rapidly, participants in the high-performance general-purpose robot sector include both emerging start-ups at home and abroad—such as Unitree Technology (宇树科技), Boston Dynamics, and Figure—as well as cross-industry enterprises such as Tesla, XPeng, and Xiaomi. Because this field involves a wide variety of technologies with high development difficulty, the overall capital and technological barriers to entry are high, and no mature general-purpose application scenarios have yet been formed. Enterprises that lack strong technological accumulation and capital support will find it difficult to develop excellent products. Consequently, leading enterprises with first-mover advantages and sustained R&D capabilities possess relatively pronounced competitive advantages at the current stage.

The downstream of the high-performance general-purpose robot industrial chain is primarily oriented toward commercial scenarios of practical application, with its core value lying in replacing or assisting humans in completing various tasks—especially in scenarios involving extremely arduous or dangerous tasks. From the perspective of application scenarios, high-performance general-purpose robots are primarily directed toward two categories: industry-level applications and consumer-level applications. At the industry-level application level, robots help address the trend of population aging and alleviate future structural labor shortages. Currently, quadruped robots have achieved initial applications in areas such as power grid inspection, fire rescue, and unmanned delivery, and enterprises in the industry are further exploring pathways for quadruped and humanoid robots to be deployed in industrial scenarios such as automotive manufacturing, petrochemical processing, and 3C manufacturing. At the consumer-level application level, high-performance general-purpose robots are expected to take on roles in public services, household services, elderly care services, and similar capacities, and are expected to generate scaled market demand in sub-sectors such as children's education, science education activities, commercial performances, and cultural exhibitions.

2. Technology Level, Market Overview, and Future Development Trends of the High-Performance General-Purpose Quadruped Robot Industry

(1) Software and Hardware Technologies Are Becoming Increasingly Mature, with Significant Advantages in Complex Environments

A quadruped robot is a bionic legged robot with extremely strong locomotion capability, designed with the concept of imitating the limb structure and gait of animals. Through highly complex mechanical structures and robust control algorithms, equipped with multiple types of sensors, actuators, and control systems, it possesses extremely strong environmental adaptability and is capable of walking stably and performing tasks across a variety of complex terrains.

In the early 21st century, advances in computer control technology gave rise to the emergence of quadruped robot prototypes, but the development of quadruped robots in this early conceptual stage was relatively slow. With the rapid development of control theory, machine learning, precision manufacturing, and precision sensors, European and American research institutions and enterprises (such as Boston Dynamics) released quadruped robot prototypes. Thereafter, Unitree Technology (宇树科技) was the first in the world to achieve the technical solution of high-performance electrically-driven quadruped robots and bring them to market commercially. In recent years, the maturation of technologies such as artificial intelligence, environmental perception, reinforcement learning, high-performance sensors, and high-computing-power chips has propelled high-performance quadruped robots toward higher levels of intelligence, while domestic enterprises have been conducting extensive technology exploration and process improvement to develop more cost-effective quadruped robot products, thereby leading the development of the high-performance quadruped robot industry. Currently, against a backdrop of "technology maturation + cost reduction," quadruped robots are becoming new-type products in fields such as scientific research, industry, fire rescue, and education and entertainment, and commercial space is gradually opening up.

At the present stage, quadruped robots possess advantages including stable and flexible capability to traverse complex terrain, load-bearing capacity, and autonomous navigation ability, which demonstrate powerful adaptability across various complex environments and constitute an important basis for their commercial applications.

(2) Commercial Applications Are Being Deployed at an Accelerating Pace, with Broad Application Prospects for the Future

Compared with bipedal humanoid robots, quadruped robots possess higher effective payloads and extremely strong balance capabilities, and are also more conducive to control, design, and maintenance; consequently, their commercial deployment has been faster. Domestic and international enterprises, through technological iteration and in-house production, have achieved mass production of quadruped robots at relatively low production costs, and quadruped robots have completed initial commercial deployment in certain scenarios (such as industrial inspection and fire rescue). Furthermore, from a technological perspective, quadruped robots and humanoid robots share a degree of technological commonality, and the technological iteration and commercialization process of quadruped robots also provides valuable technical and application experience for the future deployment of humanoid robots.

Currently, the application scenarios for quadruped robots can be divided into industry-level applications and consumer-level applications. In the short term, the commercial deployment of industry-level scenarios such as power grid inspection and fire rescue is relatively rapid; in the medium-to-long term, consumer-level commercial applications supported by a large consumer base will be one of the core drivers of future market growth.

In industry-level applications, the current main commercial deployment of quadruped robots is concentrated in intelligent inspection and emergency fire-rescue scenarios. Quadruped robots can perform equipment monitoring and condition monitoring in industrial scenarios such as petrochemicals, coal, electric power, and steel, and can carry out patrol and search-and-rescue work in emergency rescue scenarios such as firefighting and disaster relief. Traditional manual inspection and rescue operations suffer from harsh environmental conditions, high labor intensity, low efficiency, and high safety risks. Quadruped robots equipped with LiDAR, cameras, gas sensors, and other devices can navigate autonomously in complex environments, precisely collect data and monitor the environment, improve the accuracy and timeliness of inspection and rescue operations, and ensure safe facility operation and precise completion of rescue missions.

In consumer-level applications, quadruped robots can accompany the elderly and children, provide entertainment and interaction, and satisfy people's emotional needs; they can also serve as teaching aids to help students learn new knowledge through interaction. As people's living standards improve and consumption concepts evolve, consumers have an increasingly high acceptance of high-technology, intelligent, and highly interactive products, which is expected to drive explosive growth in the consumer-level market for quadruped robots. Performance and cost are important variables affecting commercial deployment in the consumer sector. In terms of performance, natural human-robot interaction capability is an essential skill for quadruped robots to enter households; the development of embodied large models is expected to achieve the embedding of embodied intelligence at the model level, enabling quadruped robots to understand human behavior and make autonomous decisions, thereby truly realizing their positioning as household companions. In terms of cost, the retail price of consumer-level quadruped robots has already fallen below RMB 10,000; as production volumes expand, costs are expected to continue to decline under large-scale production, at which point quadruped robots will truly begin to enter households and become a new type of consumer product in the fields of education, entertainment, and companionship.

(3) Market Scale Is Continuously Expanding, with Domestic Enterprises Holding a Far Leading Share

Although quadruped robot technology is currently still mainly in the R&D stage and being trialed in special scenarios, with continuous technology maturation, cost reduction, and expansion of application scenarios, the price of quadruped robots will continue to decline and the overall market scale will continue to grow.

According to forecasts by industry research institutions, by 2030 global quadruped robot sales are expected to exceed 560,000 units, and the global quadruped robot market size is expected to exceed RMB 8 billion; by 2030, quadruped robot sales in China will approach 400,000 units, accounting for approximately 71% of global sales, and China's quadruped robot market size in 2030 is expected to exceed RMB 4.8 billion, accounting for approximately 60% of the global market. China will become the world's largest market for quadruped robots.

According to estimates by industry research institutions, the global quadruped robot market size in 2024 was approximately USD 180 million, with products covering consumer-grade and commercial-grade quadruped robots, and sales shipments of approximately 20,000 units. Among these, consumer-grade quadruped robots dominated in terms of shipment volume, accounting for 72.1% of total market shipments, primarily applied in scenarios such as education and training and entertainment, and drove technology popularization and user awareness through strong accessibility and interactivity. Although commercial-grade quadruped robots shipped in smaller quantities, their high requirements for intelligence, environmental adaptability, and system reliability result in significantly higher per-unit added value, and they have achieved initial deployment in high-intensity scenarios such as university research, electric power, and oil and gas.

Currently, the global quadruped robot industry remains in the early commercial stage of technology exploration and has not yet achieved large-scale applications, but stimulated by potential market space, the number of enterprises and institutions deploying quadruped robots globally continues to increase. The main participants in the global quadruped robot industry currently include manufacturers such as Unitree Technology (宇树科技), Boston Dynamics, and ANYbotics. Among them, domestic manufacturers have demonstrated outstanding performance in technological innovation, cost control, and adaptability to the local market, and by continuously developing new products and services and actively expanding consumer-grade and industry-grade market share, have gradually come to dominate the major market share of global quadruped robots.

From the overall competitive landscape, the global quadruped robot market is gradually being dominated by domestic manufacturers. The Company, with its advantages of high performance and high cost-effectiveness, has gained widespread recognition among global users, demonstrating leading international competitiveness, with its quadruped robot market share ranking first globally for multiple consecutive years.

3. Technology Level, Market Overview, and Future Development Trends of the High-Performance General-Purpose Humanoid Robot Industry

(1) The Technology Innovation System Has Been Initially Established; Key Core Technologies Still Require Further Breakthroughs

The development of the humanoid robot industry requires synergistic innovation and joint breakthroughs in advanced technologies such as artificial intelligence, high-end manufacturing, and new materials. According to the Guiding Opinions on the Innovative Development of Humanoid Robots issued by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, by 2025, an initial innovation system for humanoid robots shall be established, and a number of key technologies relating to the "brain," "cerebellum," and "body" shall achieve breakthroughs, ensuring the safe and effective supply of core components and assemblies. In this context, the "brain," "cerebellum," and "body" are essentially analogous descriptions of the overall functional architecture of a general-purpose robot, with primary functions as follows:

Primary Architecture Primary Functions
"Brain" Responsible for perceiving the external environment and simulating the human cognitive decision-making process; primary functions are environmental understanding, intelligent interaction, and cognitive reasoning. The foundation is the development of multimodal large models composed of machine vision, large language models, and similar technologies. Typically refers to the cognitive intelligence or embodied large model that enables robots to achieve understanding-interaction, autonomous decision-making, and task planning capabilities.
"Cerebellum" Mimics biological organisms to perform complex motion control; primary functions are motion control, path planning, and gait balance. Typically refers to the proprioceptive intelligence or embodied proprioceptive intelligence model that enables robots to achieve high-stability, high-complexity, high-dynamic-response motion control capabilities.
"Body" Contains the robot's physical structure and actuators; performs precise servo closed-loop control over each motor in an efficient and accurate manner according to the motion plans from the cerebellum layer, completing specified motion tasks.

Among these, the "brain" technology represented by embodied large models is the cognitive capability foundation for robots to achieve understanding-interaction, autonomous decision-making, and task planning, and to enter application scenarios such as industrial production, social services, and family life at scale; the "cerebellum" technology represented by embodied proprioceptive intelligence models is the prerequisite motion capability for robots to complete various complex tasks with high stability and high flexibility.

1) Current Technology Level and Future Development Direction of the Humanoid Robot "Brain"

The humanoid robot "brain" is the core hub for achieving intelligent decision-making and environmental interaction, and its technological development is undergoing a fundamental transformation from specialized, closed, pre-programmed systems toward general-purpose, open embodied large models. This evolution is primarily built upon breakthrough advances in multimodal large model technology. At the current stage, leading research systems are dedicated to building cognitive architectures capable of deeply integrating and understanding multi-source heterogeneous information from vision, language, acoustics, and even tactile modalities. Such architectures enable robots to semantically deconstruct and model unstructured environments, and subsequently execute natural language instructions containing complex constraints and implicit premises.

At the system architecture level, in order to balance the universality of high-level cognition with the real-time performance and reliability of low-level control, the hierarchical collaborative framework of "brain-cerebellum" has become one of the mainstream technology pathways. Within this framework, the high-level "brain" undertakes macro-cognitive functions such as symbolic reasoning, task planning, and scene understanding, with its output being abstract action intentions and target states; the low-level "cerebellum" specializes in translating these abstract intentions into specific joint-space trajectories, torque control commands, and handling low-level issues such as dynamic constraints and balance maintenance.

In addition, end-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, as an emerging technology pathway, are receiving widespread attention. This type of model aims to bypass the traditional explicit state estimation and symbolic planning stages, attempting to establish a direct mapping from high-dimensional perception inputs to low-level control instructions, demonstrating good generalization capability when handling unknown tasks, and possessing good development prospects.

Furthermore, with breakthroughs in technologies such as video generation, spatiotemporal modeling, and multimodal fusion, "world models" have become one of the most promising technology pathways in the field of embodied intelligence. Their core significance lies in endowing agents with an "inherent understanding and simulation capability of the world"—the ability to extract physical laws, causal structures, and environmental dynamics from high-dimensional, multimodal perceptual data corresponding to the agent's interactions with the environment, thereby forming internal representations that can be used for reasoning and planning, and thus improving decision-making performance. This has given rise to two core functions of "world models." The first is as a simulation engine, enabling robots to perform internal future scenario prediction and task rehearsal without relying entirely on real-world trial and error, providing synthetic data, significantly reducing data collection costs and improving strategy iteration speed. The second is strategy enhancement, building an architecture paradigm of "World-Model–Action (WMA)," enabling agents, in the course of interacting and operating with the environment, not only to understand "what is happening now," but more critically to predict "what will happen next," thereby supporting decision models in generating safer, more efficient, and more universally applicable action strategies. The gradual maturation of this WMA model technology is expected to lead a fundamental leap in embodied intelligence technical capabilities.

In addition to VLA models and WMA models, dual-system architecture is also one of the important technology pathways in the current field of embodied intelligence. This architecture mimics the human "fast and slow thinking" cognitive mode, using multimodal large models as the slow system to provide cross-scenario generalized perception and planning capabilities, and using VLA/action expert strategies as the fast system to ensure real-time action performance and reliability, while also possessing advantages such as interpretability and interventability of the decision-making process and independent iterative upgrading of each sub-system. Although efficient collaboration between the fast and slow systems still faces certain technical bottlenecks, the dual-system architecture, with its clear functional division and prominent scalability, is still regarded as one of the most promising technology pathways in the field of embodied intelligence.

Currently, embodied large models worldwide are in the exploratory stage of rapid technological development, and no unified, mature technological consensus or competitive landscape has yet been formed within the industry. Multiple technology pathways including VLA models, WMA models, and dual-system architectures are developing in parallel, jointly exploring the future of embodied intelligence; this situation also provides enterprises in the industry with broader space for innovation and differentiated competitive opportunities.

2) Current Technology Level and Future Development Direction of the Humanoid Robot "Cerebellum"

The humanoid robot "cerebellum" is the center of its motion control, responsible for converting the abstract motion intentions of the high-level "brain" into precise, stable, and compliant bodily movements, and for solving the problems of real-time control and dynamic balance in high-dimensional, nonlinear, underactuated dynamic systems. The current technology level has significantly surpassed the traditional control technology pathway that relied on precise analytical models and pre-programmed action libraries in the early days, and has entered a new stage of hybrid intelligent control characterized by the combination of data-driven approaches and optimization theory.

At the level of whole-body motion control, current research is mainly focused on the direction of reinforcement learning control. Reinforcement learning is an important branch of machine learning; robots continuously interact with the environment, learn through trial and error based on rewards or penalties received, and ultimately find the optimal strategy to achieve long-term goals. At the level of manipulation and execution control, the technology pathway is shifting from cumbersome manual programming reliant on expert knowledge toward autonomous manipulation paradigms based on large-scale demonstration datasets, diffusion models, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and related technologies. By deploying parallel training clusters in highly realistic physical simulation environments and using deep reinforcement learning and other algorithms to explore vast strategy spaces, robots can autonomously discover and master manipulation strategies that adapt to uncertainty in object morphology, changes in environmental parameters, and diversity in task objectives. These strategies are then effectively transferred to physical entities through system identification and domain randomization simulation-to-reality techniques.

3) Current Technology Level and Future Development Direction of the Humanoid Robot Body

The humanoid robot body is the physical carrier and execution terminal through which its intelligent cognitive and motion control capabilities are realized; the comprehensive performance of its mechanical structure, drive system, sensor configuration, and material selection fundamentally sets the ceiling of the robot's overall capabilities. Currently, the development trend of body technology presents the distinct characteristic of a mutual contest and integration between the pursuit of high performance and the feasibility of industrialization.

At the level of core drive and transmission components, joint modules, as the movement core of the robot, have increasingly complex levels of technology integration. Advanced joint modules adopt a highly integrated mechatronic design, typically integrating high-power-density frameless torque motors, low-backlash high-stiffness reducers, high-resolution encoders, and joint force or torque sensors, thereby achieving high-bandwidth force-position hybrid control and collision detection capabilities. In terms of end effectors, dexterous hands have evolved from functionally simple two-finger grippers to anthropomorphic designs with multiple active degrees of freedom and integrated distributed tactile sensor skin, enabling them to perform tasks requiring fine force and attitude control, such as stably grasping fragile objects or operating specialized tools.

In terms of overall configuration and locomotion modalities, there currently exist multiple technology pathways, primarily "bipedal humanoid" with "wheel-legged hybrid" as a supplement. Among these, the bipedal humanoid configuration theoretically possesses the best morphological adaptability to the existing infrastructure of human environments, and has higher commercialization potential through the balancing and overall enhancement of technologies in areas such as dynamic balance control, energy efficiency, mechanical complexity, and manufacturing cost. The hybrid configuration of "humanoid upper body, wheeled lower body" has higher movement efficiency, longer range, better static stability, and lower commercialization cost on flat or lightly rough ground, giving it priority deployment feasibility in specific application scenarios such as logistics, guided tours, and reception services.

Looking ahead, the evolution of humanoid robot body technology will unfold around the following directions. First is the standardization of the industrial chain and the optimization and control of total lifecycle costs. Currently, the R&D and manufacturing costs of prototype machines are relatively high; promoting the standardization, modularization, and performance grading of core components (such as joint modules, sensor units, and control interfaces) is a key prerequisite for achieving large-scale mass production, reducing marginal costs, and fostering a healthy industrial ecosystem. Second is the deep empowerment of new materials and advanced manufacturing processes. To achieve the balance of "lightweight," "high strength," and "high stiffness," the future will see extensive exploration and application of high-performance carbon fiber composite materials, titanium-aluminum alloys, and lightweight lattice structures and topologically optimized components achieved through additive manufacturing technology, with the aim of, while ensuring structural integrity, minimizing the rotational inertia of moving parts and overall energy consumption. Finally, there is the bionic optimization of bipedal humanoid configuration solutions, including simulating the response characteristics of biological tendons, designing passive safety structures during loss of stability, integrating high-density, multimodal (visual, tactile, force, inertial, auditory) body sensor networks, and building a redundant, reliable, real-time-sensing proprioceptive system.
